Documents AI Summary:
Background: The US Department of Agriculture, Foreign Agriculture Services (FAS) is seeking information for market research purposes to determine an acquisition strategy for purchasing services related to the Global Maximum Residue Levels (MRL) Database. 

The MRLs represent the highest acceptable amount of residual pesticides or other chemicals that can remain in agricultural commodities without posing a safety risk to consumers. This initiative aims to address the challenges posed by varying MRL standards across countries, which can inhibit trade and affect the agricultural sector.
Work Details: The contractor will build, deploy, and maintain a database of Maximum Residue Levels (MRLs) disaggregated by commodity, compound/substance, and country or market of destination. 

The database must be accessible via a web-based portal available to USDA users at all times. It should include data on at least 1,000 chemical products, 900 agricultural commodities, and 150 destination markets. The contractor must ensure that any changes in MRLs are reflected within 14 days and validated semi-annually. 

The portal should allow users to export data in formats like MS Excel and provide intuitive navigation without requiring specialized knowledge. Additionally, the contractor must compile information from various sources to ensure accuracy and provide user guides and technical support.
Place of Performance: The work will primarily be performed through a web-based portal accessible by USDA personnel.

Solicitation Title: PNW 5C FFS Wildland-Urban Interface Risk Approaches
Description Text:
This project aims to develop and deliver a database of wildfire-affected building footprints, classified by building type, attributed with structure separation distance (SSD), construction materials, and uncertainty categorized for this data. The database will cover significant spatiotemporal extents (e.g., 2010 to 2022 in the U.S.) and include unique information from the National Fire Incident Reporting Systems and other databases. High-resolution (e.g., 1 meter) pre-fire and post-fire-affected vegetation datasets will be derived at select fires. We will use this data to assess the uncertainty in correlation studies. We will focus on examining relationships between pre-fire and fire-affected vegetation, relationships between defensive actions and property treatments (e.g., defensible space), and how conclusions of correlation studies change when using new data derived through this project. Project data will support examining current WUI definitions when considering SSD and fine-scale vegetation. Additionally, we will evaluate WUI fire behavior models. These efforts will facilitate examining and enhancing current parcel and building-scale risk assessment approaches.

The goal is to provide valuable data that can inform risk assessment approaches and improve fire behavior models.
Work Details: The contractor will be responsible for several key tasks: 
1. Integrate available data from large wildland fires into the database. 
2. Audit data from other SERDP projects to ensure quality and relevance. 
3. Monitor and evaluate the potential for data integration from other projects.

Description Text:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source: USDA-ERS, Comprehensive Historical Data for Agricultural Products



United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economic Research Services (ERS), Market and Trade Economics Division (MTED) intends to award on a sole source basis a Firm Fixed Price Purchase Order to Sitonia Consulting Limited.



The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 518210 – Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services. Analytical Laboratory Instrument Manufacturing with a Size Standard of $40 million.



Sitonia Consulting Limited (SCL) is an independent market analysis firm focused on Chinese agricultural markets based out of Dublin, Ireland. SCL has many years of experience analyzing and reporting on Chinese agricultural futures markets. SCL is the only vendor that has researchers who can produce an extensive, query-able database that includes Chinese agricultural market prices, with an extensive history that will be used for data analysis for ERS products. They have the expertise in collection and analysis of Chinese market data and develop a query-able data base that meets ERS requirement to use in analysis of futures, options, and cash prices of grains, oilseeds, animal feed and meat markets for a study of China’s futures markets. No other known sources have the capabilities to provide this extensive of data required in SOW.

United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economic Research Services (ERS), Resource and Rural Economics Division (RRED) intends to award on a sole source basis a Firm Fixed Price Purchase Order to Randall-Reilly, LLC dba Fusable.



The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this requirement is 518210 – Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services. Analytical Laboratory Instrument Manufacturing with a Size Standard of $40 million.



Randall-Reilly, LLC, d/b/a Fusable has an Equipment Data Associates (EDA) Iron Solutions product. Background: The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), specifically the Economic Research Services (ERS) within the Resource and Rural Economics Division (RRED), is seeking to enhance its agricultural economic research capabilities. The agency aims to understand the impact of government payments, crop insurance, and macroeconomic factors such as interest rates on the equipment market. 

This contract is intended to provide proprietary data that will bridge gaps in existing survey data and offer deeper insights into market dynamics and regional investment behaviors.
Work Details: The contractor shall provide comprehensive datasets including: 
1. Quarterly historical data for wholesale and resale agricultural equipment and machinery values from 2008 to 2024, disaggregated by geographic region. This includes a summary guide of all Iron values with breakdowns by US regions (Southwest, Southeast, Northwest, North Central). Data attributes must include issue name, quarter, region description, published date, equipment type, make, model, year, average wholesale price, trade rough value, trade premium value, and resale cash price.
2. Quarterly historical data on the total volume of new and used equipment and machinery financed from 2008 to present. This data must be categorized by state and include counts for new and used equipment sold, leased, or rented.
3. Estimated capture rates of all equipment in Uniform Commercial Code filings classified by equipment codes with descriptions and estimated capture rates.
Period of Performance: One year from the date of award.

FastMarkets Global Limited (FGL) provides daily spot price assessments of Soybean Meal, Sunseeds, Barley, Rape seed, Rape Meal; it is the only agency producing daily forward curve assessments of the wheat, corn and soybean markets. For example, searching for global wheat data, a few vendors do provide such data, but they do not also provide corn, soybean, barley. Solicitation Title: USDA FSA - Dairy Margin Coverage - Decision Tool - Maintenance
Description Text:
Under Statutory Authority 41 U.S.C. 1901, the United States Department of Agriculture, Farm Production and Conservation – Business Center intends to award a sole source commercial service firm-fixed price contract to Bozic LLC for Dairy Margin Coverage (DMC) that provides risk management for participating dairy operations. The DMC Cairy Decision Tool is an online resource used by participating dairy operations for margin selection in annual DMC enrollment and provides information regarding milk price and feed cost trends.

This website has been in use since 2021 and Bozic LLC has been the original developer and sole provider of maintenance and support services since its implementation.  The DMC Decision Tool was created as a resource for dairy operations considering participation in the DMC risk management program. Dairy economists from land grant universities developed the tool because of their expertise in the concept of the dairy margin (the difference between the monthly National All-Milk price and feed cost formula including corn, soybean meal and sand premium alfalfa hay).

Bozic LLC is uniquely qualified to provide maintenance services for The DMC Decision Tool website due to the following reasons:


	Bozic LLC has in-depth understanding of the website’s architecture, customizations, and operational nuances developed over the years.
	The technical staff at Bozic LLC has specialized skills and training that are critical for effective maintenance and support, which cannot be replicated by other vendors without significant time and cost investment.
	Engaging a different vendor for the DMC Decision Tool would pose significant risks
	
		Operational disruptions, transitioning maintenance services to a new vendor could lead to operational disruptions and potential downtime, impacting critical business functions.
		Quality and reliability would cause a risk of degraded quality and reliability to support service delivers due to the new vendor’s learning curve.
	
	
	Transitions costs, switching to a new vendor would incur substantial transitions costs, including training, knowledge transfer, and potential modifications to the DMC Decision Tool to accommodate the new maintenance provider.


This acquisition is conducted under the authority of 41 U.S.C. 1901 Simplified Acquisition procedures as implemented in FAR 13.106-3(B)(3)(i), only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.  This will be a firm fixed price Blanket Purchase Agreement for 60 months of performance.  The NAICS Code is 518210 Data Processing, Hosting and Related Services, $35,000,00.00 in annual revenue.

A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed action based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

Interested parties may identify their  and capability by responding to this notice via email to the Contracting Officer by 2:30 PM (EST) 8/14/2024.

Capability statements shall be submitted by email only as a MS Word or Adobe PDF attachment to Oriel Velez-Olivieri, at oriel.velez@usda.gov and Kristina Baker, at Kristina.baker@usda.gov.

TYPE IN THE SUBJECT LINE “12FPC424Q0114 DCM Tool”.  TELEPHONIC INQUIRIES WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.

All information submitted should support the offerors capability to provide the product required and shall be furnished at no cost or obligation to the Government.  The Government reserves the right to cancel to Sole Source Award.  If no responses are received, the Contracting Officer will proceed with the sole source to Bozic LLC.

Description Text:
We intend to Sole Source this requirement to Critical Mention INC. PWS below, no other vendors that complete all task ahve been found, 

PERFORMANCE WORK STATEMENT (PWS)
Office of External Affairs
News Monitoring and Metric Service
USDA Rural Development

1.0 PROGRAM AND MISSION BACKGROUND
1.1 Rural Development (RD) works to foster opportunity and economic security in rural America by investing in local infrastructure, supporting a community’s people and jobs, and helping advance rural prosperity with access to innovation and technology. Through loans, grants and partnerships with local leaders, RD provides tools and resources to help ensure rural families, businesses, and communities have the support they need to prosper, today and into the future. RD is a catalyst for rural prosperity by improving high-speed internet access, helping to provide affordable rural housing, supporting the connection of rural skillsets to jobs of the future; aiding in the modernization of roads, bridges, and water systems; and assisting communities’ access to healthcare.
1.2 The Office of External Affairs (OEA) is the central point of contact for USDA Rural Development (RD) on public affairs and Congressional relations, including press and social media. OEA’s major functions include: coordinate the development and dissemination of information to the press on RD programs and policies; coordinate Congressional relations and Congressional correspondence; oversee the development and maintenance of RD’s external website and social media platforms; review and disseminate internal RD employee communications; manage RD’s conference and event planning activities; and, coordinate the production of multimedia, publications, and printed materials. All OEA functions are performed in support of RD’s work to foster opportunity and economic security in rural America.
2.0 News Monitoring and Metric Service Scope of Effort
Within the function of the development and dissemination of information to the press on RD programs and policies, OEA monitors the results of these efforts.
The monitoring of these efforts consists of several components: the location and identification of earned media placements; the collection of those placements; the organization of those placements; the redistribution of those placements; the analysis of those placements.
The results of the monitoring has multiple constituencies. One group is all RD employees and contractors. In effect, anyone on an RD ListServ. A second group is leadership from OEA, RD and USDA. A third group is key Members of Congress and selected staff. The final group is external stakeholders and partners.
Satisfying the components of the monitoring mentioned above requires OEA to have tools that are simultaneously accurate, comprehensive, and efficient, since the breadth of media interest on RD programs and policies covers all states and territories. That media ranges from sophisticated, digital-first operations with dozens of staff and a broad audience, to weekly or even monthly publications that are trade-centric or hyper-local, with perhaps one or two employees.
The aforementioned constituencies are similarly diverse in their expectations as will be detailed below.
3.0 PROGRAM OBJECTIVES

The curation and publication of the daily NewScan product requires that OEA locate and identify media, collect the media, and organize the media -- or three of the four components of service. These components reach all the constituencies referenced above.
The analysis component of service is of use to the OEA press team, OEA leadership, RD leadership, and USDA leadership for strategic purposes.
4.0 DESCRIPTION OF PROGRAM TASK SUPPORT REQUIREMENTS
4.0.1 OEA achieves the first part of the monitoring – locating and identifying – through a complex search function. The complexity is necessitated by the scope of RD’s operations, the scope of the interest in those operations and the range of entities that publish news on those operations.
Due to the volume of media created on a daily basis in response to RD’s programs and/or principles, it is critical that the search function be as frictionless as possible in that it is easy to read, easy to ascertain the relevance of the media in question and be easy to flag as being relevant.
Once potential RD-related media hits are located, they must be identified with certainty as being about RD’s programs and/or principles. Once that certainty has been met, all media published within the past 24 hours is then assembled and put into digital space and reviewed further. It is essential that this digital space be secure and high in capacity, preferably in the “cloud.”
The media in that digital space represents the media that is subject to analysis, a point to which will be returned to in 4.0.2.
The media that rests in this digital space is now eligible to be considered for that day’s NewScan. Due to the volume referenced earlier, it is not practical to include every piece of media in this space in that day’s NewScan. Media that captures the thrust of the most recent press outreach is included, with every effort made to limit duplicative media while assuring that what is included is comprehensive and satisfies the varied interests of the core constituencies of NewScan: RD employees and contractors, key Members of Congress and selected staff, and external stakeholders and partners.
Of note, some media in this digital space may be tangentially about RD or subjective in tone. Such media may be of interest of to various members of leadership, but not suitable for the core constituencies of NewScan.
Now that the media had been collected into one spot, it must be redistributed via NewScan.
In order for the media to be redistributed, it must be edited. Broadcast (Radio and TV) clips are edited to include the portion relevant to RD. Text/online clips are edited to include the key passage or relevant quote.
Here again, it is paramount that the tools used to perform the editing are efficient and accurate. This is the most labor-intensive element to the process.

Once the editing is complete the media must be organized. Typically, it is organized by subject matter or by principal at the National level. Outside this milieu, media is organized alphabetically by state.
The organization component requires tools that are flexible enough to manipulate a variety of media, while also maintaining the visual integrity of the media that has been identified for inclusion in that day’s NewScan.
Once the organization is complete, the last component – redistribution to the aforementioned constituencies – is next.
This component is dependent on a tool that can generate source code that is a) mobile-friendly b) easily navigable within the MS Outlook setting c) of high visual integrity regardless of the platform of the user.
The constituencies may have different expectations. RD employees may be interested to see that their work is receiving positive publicity. Stakeholders may wish to see that their priorities are being well-received by RD and other partners. Members of Congress and staff may wish to assure that taxpayer dollars are being used properly.

The digital space referenced above is the holding area for all media related to RD collected in the past 24 hours. It is essential that as much of this media contain key data points such as: Name of Publication
Time and Date of Publication Location of Publication
Author Estimated Audience
Length of media (word count or time, in the case of broadcast)
The media in this space is then copied to a separate space dependent on: the time it was published, the principal involved, the subject matter, the location or other factors as identified by leadership.
When this media is collected at scale – either over a month, or in the week subsequent to press outreach – trends can be identified by leadership to assess the effectiveness of media strategy with the respect to number of people reached, the location of those people and other factors as identified by leadership.

Description Text:
This is a Notice of Proposed Contract Action Synopsis. There is no solicitation available at this time. Requests for a solicitation will receive no response. This Proposed Contract Action is published to notify potential sources that the National Forest Service, Rocky Mountain Research Station Missoula MT, intends to award a sole source firm-fixed price contract, on behalf of the USDA Forest Service, to Data2Bio, for genotyping-by-sequencing (tGBS) of Moose DNA samples in accordance with the attached statement of work.

Data2Bio developed innovative technology (tGBS) that provides a higher accuracy of genotyping SNP data.   Data2Bio has been a pioneer in the realm of DNA Genomic Sequencing and with the numerous laboratory steps involved.  This allows the Government to ensure all data processing required to obtain SNP genotyping data from the starting DNA that will be provided, and they are able to guarantee the Government receives high quality SNP genotype data with minimal missing data. The responses to this synopsis will be used to confirm that this is a sole source requirement. Documents AI Summary:
Background: The National Forest Service, Rocky Mountain Research Station Missoula MT, intends to award a sole source firm-fixed price contract, on behalf of the USDA Forest Service, to Data2Bio for genotyping-by-sequencing (tGBS) of Moose DNA samples. Data2Bio has developed innovative technology (tGBS) that provides a higher accuracy of genotyping SNP data. The goal of the project is to generate large-scale genome data for DNA extracts of moose for the purpose of conducting population genetic structure analysis.
Work Details: The technical methodologies to be used include evaluating DNA concentration, preparing DNA samples, constructing DNA sequencing libraries, generating raw DNA sequence data using Next Generation Sequencing, performing barcode sorting to demultiplex samples, identifying polymorphic DNA sites (SNPs), aligning and trimming DNA reads from each sample, genotyping using the identified SNPs, and providing a summary report 10-14 weeks from sample submission.

The deliverables include notification of DNA sample quality 3 weeks after samples are received, a final summary report 10-16 weeks after samples are received, and data files containing all SNP data and accompanying DNA sequencing files 10-16 weeks after samples are received.
Place of Performance: Missoula MT

In order to hunt deer or turkeys during a quota hunt on Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area (LBL), a hunter must apply and be drawn for a quota hunt permit. The Contractor shall supply and administer a developed, tested, and proven online Quota Hunt Application System (QHAS) that will accept applications, collect application fees, conduct a randomized quota hunt drawing with preference points, send email notices to all registrants, sell LBL permits online, and allow hunters to check their application history, preference points, and print hunting information, permits, maps, etc. 

The website shall collect applicant information and store it in a secure environment that meets the requirements of the U.S. Government. After the close of each application period, the Contractor will conduct a randomized quota hunt drawing and assign permits or preference points to applicants. Contractor shall maintain a secure database with the most recent 5 years of application history, which shall be the property of the Forest Service (FS), and shall be submitted to FS as requested and at the conclusion of the contract. 

During the application periods (currently, the month of February for turkey hunts and the month of July for deer hunts) and throughout the year, the Contractor and Forest Service (FS) designated staff will have real-time access to applicant data for corrections to hunter information, additions, deletions, and to assist hunters in the registration process if needed.

Contractor will make quota hunt drawing results available to applicants through the website and by email. At the conclusion of each drawing, all applicants will receive email notification with their drawing results, printable permit if drawn, and new preference point balance.  Also, applicants can log back into the website at any time to check their group number, draw status, preference point balance for deer or turkeys, print a quota hunt permit if drawn, print their application history for the past 5 years, and purchase and print related permits (e.g. Hunter Use Permit, Backcountry Camping Permit, etc.).

The website shall be attractive, convenient and easy to use whether accessing with a mobile device or personal computer, accept credit card and debit card payments, and enable hunters to check draw status, preference points, and purchase and print permits.

Note: rather than develop a new system, we are looking for an already developed, tested, and proven system that can be adapted to most closely meet the needs of our visitors and the U.S. Forest Service.

Description Text:
SNAP ELECTRONIC BENEFITS TRANSFER (EBT) GATEWAY SERVICES



INTRODUCTION

This SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE is to determine the availability of potential small businesses (e.g., 8(a), service-disabled veteran owned small business, HUBZone small business, small disadvantaged business, veteran-owned small business, and women-owned small business) that can provide:

SNAP Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) Gateway Services



The information from this market research will help USDA/Food Nutrition Service plan their acquisition strategy.  Please be sure to indicate if you have a GSA schedule contract, a contract on GSA 8(a) STARS, GSA VETS GWAC, or none.

THIS IS STRICTLY MARKET RESEARCH.  USDA/ Procurement Operations Division WILL NOT ENTERTAIN QUESTIONS REGARDING THIS MARKET RESEARCH.

                                                                                               

BACKGROUND

FNS is responsible for policy related to SNAP retailer management and EBT benefit issuance; systems and contracts used to manage retailer participation and compliance; the operational and technical aspects of EBT issuance; and performing the final USDA administrative review of appeals on SNAP retailer-related decisions. The ability to see transactions in real time through access to the EBT Gateways would enhance FNS’s ability to detect and fight fraud, assist in disaster relief efforts, and give FNS visibility into system outages.  FNS is referring to this access to EBT Gateways as Gateway Access Terminals (GATs).

Currently, FNS is unable to see individual transactions until two days after they occur. Post pilot assessment, a GAT would provide FNS the ability to reject potentially fraudulent transactions as they occur, as opposed to reviewing data after the fact to identify fraud. Additionally, FNS’s disaster relief efforts would benefit from seeing real-time activity by allowing FNS to see when stores have stopped conducting transactions, as well as when they are once again able to resume transacting.



RESPONSE INFORMATION

In order to respond to this notice, contractors must be able to clearly convey its experience and/or ability to perform the following tasks identified. 


	Provide FNS with EBT Gateway Administrative Terminal (GAT) access, which will include EBT transaction monitoring capabilities, monthly reports, and ability to export/pull data and reports detailed in Section 3 – Scope. The full requirements for these tasks are defined in the RTM in Attachment A (attachment to Sources Sought).


Each response should include the following Business Information:
 

a.       UEI SAM.

b.       Company Name.

c.       Company Address.

d.       Indicate if the company has a Current GSA Schedules or no contract vehicle in accordance with this Sources Sought.

f.       Type of Company (i.e., small business, 8(a), woman owned, veteran owned, etc.) as validated via the System for Award Management (https://sam.gov/content/home )  

g.       Company Point of Contact, Phone and Email address

Teaming Arrangements:  All teaming arrangements should also include the above-cited information and certifications for each entity on the proposed team. Teaming arrangements are encouraged.

Responses must be submitted not later than 12pm MST, 7/24/2023. Capability statements will not be returned and will not be accepted after the due date.  The maximum number of pages for submission is 8 pages. They must be emailed to Jessica.Massey@usda.gov with the SS# JMSS01 on the subject line.

Solicitation Title: Bryant Christie Global Maximum Residue Level Database Access
Description Text: Please see Attached Combined Synopsis and Solicitation Notice for the BC Global MRL Database Access.
Documents AI Summary:
Background: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) is seeking on-demand access to up-to-date, comprehensive information on maximum residue levels (MRLs) for agricultural products worldwide, with a particular emphasis on trade in specialty crops. MRLs represent the highest acceptable amount of residual pesticide or veterinary drug that a country allows to remain present in an agricultural commodity. The international Codex Alimentarius recommends science-based standards for MRLs on a commodity-by-commodity basis, but individual countries retain the right to set their own MRL limits. The increasing divergence of national standards and greater enforcement of maximum residue levels creates challenges for the international agricultural marketplace.
Work Details: The contractor will provide a subscription to a database of current, pending, and proposed MRLs, disaggregated by commodity, compound/substance, and country or market of destination. The data shall be inclusive of pesticide MRLs established by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), veterinary drug MRLs established by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), and foreign market MRLs. The portal shall be available for access from any web connection and should be intuitive and easy to navigate. Additionally, the contractor will provide quarterly reports on system use and may be required to participate in meetings or discussions up to four times per year.
Period of Performance: Base Year: 08/13/2023 – 08/12/2024; Option Year 1: 08/13/2024 – 08/12/2025; Option Year 2: 08/13/2025 – 08/12/2026; Option Year 3: 08/13/2026 – 08/12/2027; Option Year 4: 08/13/2027 – 08/12/2028
Place of Performance: All work will be performed consistent with the offeror’s normal business practices and policies; work at government facilities will not be authorized.

Description Text:
Mainframe as a Service - The Digital Infrastructure Services Center's (DISC) Enterprise Hosting Services (EHS) within the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) is charged with offering cost competitive, multi cloud (on prem and commercial), automated enterprise shared hosting services to USDA and other federal government organizations 24 hours a day, 7 days a week continuously. DISC is a Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) certified USDA/government owned service provider funded through the USDA's working capital fund focused on hosting services, customer service, agile operations, creative and innovative solutions, and cost control, with this effort specifically mainframe services. Currently, DISC receives approximately 40 million dollars annually from customers for mainframe support.

DISC operates from two physical and virtual locations. The primary physical location is the USDA data center in Kansas City, Missouri, with a secondary datacenter facility in St. Louis, Missouri. The secondary datacenter facility is currently in the process of being relocated to Chicago, IL. The USDA and other Federal partners depend upon Digital Infrastructure Service Center's highly complex and interconnected technology infrastructure to conduct their mainframe operations and are relying on DISC to modernize and meet their future needs utilizing a managed service model. DISC has been utilizing a managed service model, specifically mainframe as a service, since 2018, which has resulted in numerous benefits for DISC. As part of the Mainframe as a service (MaaS) the vendor assumes the risk of purchasing the hardware and hardware maintenance knowing the government 'could' choose not to renew the options which mitigates a significant amount of risk. Additionally, utilizing this managed service provides additional advantages to the DISC business model. As a working capital fund it is imperative that DISC procures a solution that is scalable and agile as the Government onboards additional agencies to their service.

The purpose of this procurement is to procure an agile scalable solution to provide (DISC) Enterprise Hosting Services (EHS) Data Center IT Mainframe Services within DISC and their external customers. The managed services shall provide 24x7/365 IT service operations. It is DISC's assumption that the vendor will collaborate with the Government and sell DISC's services to new federal customers. To accommodate the new onboards the Government has added numerous onboarding CLINS for growth. DISC anticipates exceptional growth in Mainframe services over the 10-year period of performance.

Solicitation Title: Macroeconomic Forecasting and Analysis of Macroeconomic Developments Affecting U.S. Agriculture Subscription
Description Text:
THIS NOTICE OF INTENT IS NOT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS.

This notice is for informational purposes only and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government. Vendors that believe they can provide the services listed above are invited to submit substantiating documentation in writing to the identified point of contact.  This procurement will be processed pursuant to FAR part 12 and Part 13.5. No solicitation will be issued.

Information received will be considered solely for the purposes of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. Oral communications are not acceptable in response to this notice. A determination by the Government to compete this proposed contract based on responses to this notice is solely at the discretion of the Government. Responses are to be received within 10 days of this notice. No telephone calls are being accepted.

The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economics Research Service (ERS), intends to negotiate and award on a Justification for Other Than Full and Open Competition (JOFOC) basis. A purchase order will be one year firm-fixed price (plus four option years) awarded to Oxford Economics USA, Inc. of Wayne, PA for Macroeconomic Forecasting and Analysis of Macroeconomic Developments Affecting U.S. Agriculture Subscription.

Anticipated Date of Award::  05.09.2023

Several factors make Oxford Economics USA, Inc. eligible to meet ERS's Macroeconomic Forecasting and Analysis of Macroeconomic Developments Affecting U.S. Agriculture subscription requirement:  

a. Fully integrated global economic model: Oxford's individual country models are fully linked through global assumptions about trade volumes and prices, competitiveness, capital flows, interest and exchange rates, and commodity prices.

b. Comprehensive country coverage: Eighty countries are examined in detail, including major developing countries important to USDA, such as China, India, and Brazil, as well as the European Union. The rest of the world economy is covered in six trading blocs.

c. Monthly updates: Oxford offers the latest economic data, macroeconomic forecasting, and Oxford's baseline forecast updates which are available monthly.

d. Windows-based PC software: Oxford's economic models come with software that allows ERS analysts to change model assumptions, add new variables and equations, and build their own economic models.

Description Text:
Notice of Intent to Sole Source

United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economic Research Service (ERS), seeks to obtain a one-year licenses for 30 users for an online source for daily spot price assessments of soybean meal, sunseeds, barley, rape seed, and rape meal. This information is required to cover global agriculture markets and be a part of a complete tender database with a dashboard interface for searching and downloading the data.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this project is 518210 Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services with a Size Standard of $35 million.

AgriCensus provides daily spot price assessments of Soybean Meal, Sunseeds, Barley, Rape seed, Rape Meal; it is the only agency producing daily forward curve assessments of the wheat, corn and soybean markets. AgriCensus has a complete tender database and a dashboard interface for searching and downloading the data. They also have an exports database and dashboard which can be very useful. No comparable competitor offers as comprehensive a set of information as AgriCensus with the same scope of information at a comparable cost.

It is noted that the robust dataset for tender data by AgriCensus is not exhibited by other vendors. For example, searching for global wheat data, a few vendors do provide such data, but they do not also provide corn, soybean, barley. Various combinations of these data items return the same unsatisfactory outcome.

Solicitation Title: USDA STRATUS Program Pre-Solicitation Notice- Pool 1
Description Text:
Title 

USDA Cloud BOA Request for Information (RFI) for Pool 1 Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSP) 

Background 

The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is contemplating issuing a suite of department-wide Cloud Basic Ordering Agreements (BOA) to obtain rapid access to hyperscale cloud service providers, cloud integration and development providers, and SaaS providers/resellers. This collection of BOAs, formally known as USDA STRATUS, will provide rapid access to cloud capabilities.  

STRATUS is envisioned as consisting of three multi-award pools of services and vendors:  

Pool 1: Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSP) 

Pool 2: Cloud Integration and Development 

Pool 3: SaaS providers/resellers 

Where each pool will have multiple awardees. 

The Office of Associate Chief Information Officer (ACIO) of USDA OCIO’s Digital Infrastructure Services Center (DISC) https://www.usda.gov/disc is the program owner. DISC provides enterprise-wide IT services to USDA Mission Area (MA) (see https://www.usda.gov/our-agency/about-usda/mission-areas) customers as well as external Federal Government customers. 

Market research indicates that a limited number of sources are capable of meeting USDA’s Pool 1 Hyperscale CSP requirements. Interested parties are asked to identify their interest and capability to respond to this requirement, and provide feedback as requested below. 

Environment 

DISC serves as the broker for all cloud services within USDA and can also serve as a managed service provider for cloud, hosting and development services to internal and external customers, requiring DISC to have direct privity of contract with each qualified hyperscale Cloud Service Provider (CSP). To fulfill its managed services role in design, implementation, and operations of cloud services, DISC needs to have direct access to all relevant CSP tools and dashboards including those for pricing, ordering, and invoicing. It also needs to have direct communications with CSP pre-sales engineering and solutioning resources, and direct access to CSP technical support services throughout the delivery and ongoing support phases of its customer projects.  

For its cloud brokerage role, DISC needs to maintain centralized management of shared services while enabling distributed control within the MAs for those MAs that require it on design, architecture, and deployment. DISC needs to exert appropriate oversight and management of cloud services for the USDA including the ability to apply security policies; monitor security compliance and service usage across the network; and promulgate standardized service configurations; and to automate, to the extent possible, and distribute the account provisioning process, including the management of budgets and expenditures, from DISC to its customers. This complex environment, with DISC functioning as both a cloud broker for some customers and as a managed service provider to other customers, necessitates DISC to have direct privity of contract with the CSPs. 

Required Capabilities 

The solution must have a mechanism for activating and/or deactivating any cloud service offering for USDA. There must be a mechanism to provision cloud services based on standardized, templated configurations and security policies, as well as user-friendly mechanisms to deprovision any and/or all services. The solution must also provide object and resource access control management, including data and resource tagging for billing tracking, access control, and technical policy management. 

The solution must provide universally available, resilient cloud services that are reliable, durable, and can continue to operate despite failure of portions of the infrastructure. The infrastructure must be capable of supporting dispersed users at varied security levels. 

The solution must offer efficient self-service of cloud services enabling rapid development and deployment of new applications and advanced capabilities. Additionally, the solution must support the portability of data and applications both out of and into their solution. USDA must retain ownership of its data at all times. 

Advanced data analytics services are required that securely enable data-driven and timely decision making both within a single data domain and across data domains. Advanced data analytics capabilities must support batch and streaming analytics; predictive analytics; machine learning; and Artificial Intelligence (AI). Advanced data analytics must be available at all security levels, including disparate and disconnected environments operating on multiple datasets. These capabilities must, at a minimum, be able to import and export streaming and batch data in common data formats. 

Baseline discounts are required with transparent mechanisms to increase cost savings to USDA based on measurable and right-sized metrics for the expected range of usage over the life of the STRATUS BOA. 

Pool 1 Solicitation Approach 

Following extensive internal and external market research, including due-diligence meetings with leading hyperscale CSPs and requests for information, a limited distribution solicitation is anticipated to be issued. 



Requested Information 

Interested parties are requested to respond to the following, regarding Pool 1 as described above: 


	
	What is your company information: company name, address, UEI, point of contact, and relevant corporate website.  
	
	
	What contracting best practices (e.g., pricing structures, contract types, performance metrics, incentives, etc.) do you recommend for this requirement? 
	
	
	Explain if there are any constraints that would prevent your organization from responding to a Pool 1 solicitation based on the description above. 
	
	
	Describe your capabilities and corporate experience in providing hyperscale cloud service provisioning, including identifying relevant contracts where you have been a prime contractor providing similar services. In this response section, include a description of how your firm can meet each of the required capabilities listed above. 
	
	
	Do you have any recommendations on how best the Federal security, in particular FedRAMP requirements, should be addressed in this solicitation to enable full compliance while allowing innovative solutions to be tested and deployed? 
	
	
	Describe your approach and methodologies to define consumption and tiered pricing models that you believe best fit the complex operating environment described above; describe your capabilities in streamlining and automating ordering and invoicing data and processes. 
	




Submission Information 

Please submit responses to this request for information by email to: 

Charles Freeman, Contracting Officer 

Frederick.Freeman@usda.gov 

The deadline for submissions is Thursday, May 4, 2023, NLT 5:00 PM EDT. 

Please limit your response to no more than 30 single-spaced, single-sided 8.5”x11” pages, with a minimum 11-point font (10 point font in tables or exhibits). 

Notes 


	
	This is a request for information and is not a request for proposals. 
	
	
	No awards will be made as a result of this notice. 
	
	
	Responses will be used to inform the USDA acquisition strategy. 
	
	
	Some respondents may be invited to one-on-one information exchanges with USDA. 
	
	
	Not all respondents, and possibly no respondents, will be invited to one-on-one information exchanges with USDA. 
	
	
	Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future RFP, if any is issued. 
	
	
	This is a new contract vehicle. Similar services are currently obtained using a variety of contract vehicles, a listing of which will not be provided. 
	
	
	Industry feedback is greatly appreciated!

Description Text:
Title 

USDA STRATUS Program Pre-Solicitation Notice 

Pre-Solicitation Notice 

The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is contemplating issuing a suite of department-wide Cloud Basic Ordering Agreements (BOA) to obtain rapid access to hyperscale cloud service providers, cloud integration and development providers, and SaaS providers/resellers. This collection of BOAs, formally known as USDA STRATUS, will provide rapid access to cloud capabilities.  

 STRATUS is envisioned as consisting of three multi-award pools of services and vendors:  

Pool 1: Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSP) 

Pool 2: Cloud Integration and Development 

Pool 3: SaaS providers/resellers 

The Office of Associate Chief Information Officer (ACIO) of USDA OCIO’s Digital Infrastructure Services Center (DISC) https://www.usda.gov/disc is the program owner. DISC provides enterprise-wide IT services to USDA Mission Area (MA) (see https://www.usda.gov/our-agency/about-usda/mission-areas) customers as well as external Federal Government customers. 

One of DISC’s current offerings that is included under STRATUS is serving as a consolidated point of entry for cloud (Hyperscale / Infrastructure as a Service [IaaS] / Platform as a Service [PaaS]) procurements. This has historically been viewed as the “easy button for access to the cloud”, providing shared USDA Authority to Operate (ATO) inheritance beyond the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) controls, consistent billing and approved services, and the ability to access cloud integrator services to support USDA Mission Areas and Federal Government customers. 

As the DISC organization meets the demands of its customers, it will increasingly incorporate commercial cloud capabilities and further extend them with mature platforms and DevSecOps capabilities that improve: 


	
	Flexibility 
	
	
	Data Transparency 
	
	
	Cost Optimization 
	



	
	Responsiveness to Mission Area Needs  
	


The intent is to create efficiencies across USDA IT operations, allowing customer Associate and Assistant Chief Information Officers (CIOs) to focus on mission business application deployments and sustainment, while using DISC services supported by the STRATUS BOAs to reduce costs, better allocate resources, and continuously improve and evolve USDA’s IT enterprise to keep pace with technology and business model advancements. 

The USDA vision for how STRATUS will enhance their ability to provide services includes: 


	
	Expanding the portfolio of providing complex solutions from design/architecture to implementation and support. 
	
	
	Increasing DISC ability to quickly respond to Mission Areas and external agencies who already have a design/architecture and are looking for competitive pricing and rapid implementation. 
	



	
	Continue providing cloud brokering services for customers who want to own their own task orders but with more data transparency and shared services. 
	
	
	Identify approved sources of supply for commercial cloud landing zones, enterprise SaaS and data, geospatial, and security management services. 
	


Achieving this vision is enabled by having:  


	
	A contract solution that will serve the next ten years and beyond. 
	
	
	Established contract guardrails. 
	



	
	Malleable contracts that can evolve with maturation of governance, evolution of technology, and vendor dynamics. 
	
	
	Vision for the future captured uniformly across support contracts. 
	
	
	An ecosystem of contractors aligned to the DISC vision for information transparency and integrated efforts across contracted services regardless of contractor selection for specific tasks/requirements  
	
	
	Accessible contracts with intuitive, streamlined business processes 
	


STRATUS includes a combination of new work and the potential to shift some work from existing contract vehicles. 

Industry Engagement 

There is no formal request for information or solicitation at this time. This notice is intended to publicize USDA’s upcoming intent and prepare industry for the USDA STRATUS program and acquisition. Additional requests for information, sources sought, or other notices may be issued soon.

Solicitation Title: Partnership Essentials Training 2022 508 Compliance Document Services
Description Text:
The contractor shall make all presentations and recordings from the 2022 Partnership Essentials Training 508 compliant according to legal standards. 
The National Partnership Office held its second annual Partnership Essentials Training in March 2022 and requires all presentations and recordings from the meeting to be made 508 compliant. 

1.1	Presentations There are 10 presentations with a total of 299 slides.

1.2	Recordings There are about 740 total minutes of recorded video from the training, however, some of that time is made up of breaks, and thus not all 740 minutes are required to be made compliant.  The trainings were recorded with automatic transcription, which is largely, but not entirely, accurate.

2.0	Deliverables 

2.1	Deliverables include:
Line Item 0001 - 508 compliant National Partnership Essentials Training Presentation
Line Item 0002 - 508 compliant National Partnership Essentials Training recording

Contractor must provide separate pricing for both Line Items.

2.2	Deliverables shall be provided to the COR and Project Point of Contact via email and file-sharing service (if file size is too large for email)

2.3	Material shall be delivered in in the original format of the material, video and PowerPoint format.

3.0	Period of Performance

The period of performance of this contract is two months from the date of award. 

4.0	Government Furnished Property/Information 

No government furnished property shall be provided for this PWS.  With regards to information, the Forest Service shall provide the presentation and recording.  
All documents, materials or records provided to the Contactor, developed, or maintained by the Contractor in the performance of this contract are deemed to be property of the Government and returned upon request.  

5.0	Disclosure of Information 
Information made available to the Contractor by the Government for the performance or administration of this effort shall be used only for those purposes and shall not be used in any other way without the written agreement of the Contracting Officer.  The contractor agrees to assume responsibility for protecting the confidentiality of Government records, which are not public information.  

6.0	Administrative Considerations 
Designation of Contracting Officer's Representative (COR)

The COR is responsible for the technical direction of the contract work. In no event,

Description Text:
The purpose of this contract is for the digitalization and cataloguing of paper laboratory and administrative documents in compliance with the Federal Records Act to be available for subsequent reference by ARS staff; assistance with facility close-out activities inclusive of document destruction, laboratory preparation and property removal. 

INSTRUCTIONS FOR THE PREPARATION OF OFFERS (FAR 52.212-1 Addendum)

Offerors shall provide all requested information referenced in this section. Failure to submit ANY of the required documents listed in "Offer Submission Documents" (52.212-1 Addendum) will render your quote non-responsive and thus not eligible for award. Failure to acknowledge all amendments will render your quote non-responsive and thus not eligible for award.
Offeror Submission Documents:
1. Offeror shall provide detailed pricing breakdown in accordance with the CLIN structure and items requested.   This is found on the SF 1449.   
2. (Eval Factor #1) Vendor shall provide documentation to demonstrate recent and relevant experience in providing services of this magnitude and of various trades.  Experience in the following trades is required:  Digitalization of Records, Destruction of Records, and Disposition of Excess Property.       
3. (Evaluation Factor #2) Vendor shall provide specifications for the shredder they intend to purchase and certification that the specifications meet the requirements of 32 CFR Part 2002 for the destruction of sensitive unclassified information.
4. (Evaluation Factor #3) Submit a resume for the individual projected to serve as the Senior Analyst.  The resume must be accompanied by a letter of intent signed by that individual stating their intent to work for this project if your company is awarded.  The resume must indicate three years or more of recent and relevant experience in the oversight of a project of similar magnitude.  
5. (Evaluation Factor #4) Vendor must certify that it presently has staff available that have passed a NACIC background investigation and can participate in the personnel reliability program at PIADC and maintain Aphis select agent registration.  Vendor must certify that its proposed candidates are able to commute to PIADC on a government-operated ferry departing from either Old Saybrook, CT or Orient, NY. 


FAR 52.212-2 Evaluation of Quotes- Addendum 
The Government will award a contract resulting from this solicitation to the responsible offeror whose offer conforming to the solicitation will be most advantageous to the Government, price and other factors considered. The following factors shall be used to evaluate offers: 
Lowest Price Technically Acceptable
Technically Acceptable offer is defined as either: 
Evaluation Factor #1. Failure to provide documentation that would demonstrate recent and relevant in the required trades will cause the vendors quote to be rated unacceptable. 
Evaluation Factor #2.  Failure to provide shredder specifications or failure to certify its compliance will cause the vendors quote to be rated unacceptable.  
Evaluation Factor #3. Failure to submit a resume of an individual indicating three or more years of recent and relevant experience, along with a letter of intent, shall cause the vendors quote to be rated unacceptable.        
Evaluation Factor #4. Failure to submit certification that proposed staff have passed a NACIC background investigation and participate in the reliability programs at PIADC, can maintain APHIS select agent registration, and willing to commute by government ferry daily will cause the vendor to be rated unacceptable.

Description Text:
Sources Sought Notice for Special Collections Librarian

This is a Sources Sought Notice issued by the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) for informational and planning purposes only. This is NOT a solicitation, nor is it an announcement for a forthcoming solicitation. Submission of any information in response to this notification is purely voluntary. The Government assumes no financial responsibility for any costs incurred in providing a response. This notice does not constitute a Request for Bid, Request for Quote or Request for Proposal, nor does it restrict the Government as to the ultimate acquisition approach. Responses to this notification will help to determine the acquisition strategy for any future solicitation. 
The information from this notice will help the United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA), National Agricultural Library (NAL) plan their acquisition strategy.

NAL is seeking a contractor to perform the following:

Background
The National Agricultural Library (NAL) serves as the Nation's chief agricultural library information resource and service.  NAL houses a collection of over 8.5 million items on agriculture and related disciplines.  As a national library, NAL develops and provides agricultural information products and services to agencies of the USDA and the Federal government, as well as public and private institutions and individuals worldwide. The mission of the Data Production Division (DPD) is to acquire, maintain and provide access to the NAL collection in both its physical and digital forms.  

The Special Collections Unit is responsible for acquiring, preserving, and providing access to specialized materials (including historic papers and rare books) documenting agricultural history.  Special Collections provides information services to USDA personnel, researchers, scholars, and the public.  Activities include preparing collections for public access, monitoring the use of rare and unique materials in the Special Collections reading room, and creating and participating in outreach opportunities through exhibits and tours.

Purpose and Scope
The National Agricultural Library requires the continuation of the project to establish and implement the curation and preservation of the U.S. Forest Service's Smokey Bear Collection. This project includes developing a digital collection, configuring ArchivesSpace for management of that collection, producing digital and physical products in support of exhibition, outreach and networking with several related communities, and preparing for the 80th anniversary of the Smokey Bear program.

The contractor shall provide services as described in this Statement of Work to support curation and preservation of the Smokey Bear collection and to improve access to collection materials. The contractor shall participate in customer service and reference activities to understand the needs of NAL's researchers and better serve those needs when improving collection access points.

Technical and Specific Requirements
See Attached Statement of Work

SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
Interested vendors are invited to submit a response to this Sources Sought Notification no later than 4:00 p.m. on 14 July 2022, to the attention of Sarah E. Zhang, Contract Specialist, at sarah.zhang@usda.gov. The email subject line must specify "Sources Sought for Special Collections Librarian". 
Responses should be no more than 10 pages and include the following information:
1.	Organization Name; 
2.	Contact Name, e-mail address and phone number; 
3.	Organizations UEI number; 
4.	GSA Schedule you participate in;
5.	Business size status for the applicable NAICS code; 
6.	A statement of capability with appropriate detail how the vendor intends to fulfill the requirements listed within the statement of work.
Interested vendors are instructed to provide only the data requested. No proprietary, classified, confidential, or sensitive information should be included in your response. The Government reserves the right to use any nonproprietary technical information in any resultant solicitation(s). Any additional information will not be reviewed by the USDA.
NO TELEPHONE REQUESTS WILL BE ACCEPTED.
DISCLAIMER:
This Sources Sought Notice is not a RFQ and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or ultimately award a contract. Responses will not be considered as quotes, nor will any award be made as a result of this notice. Description Text:
**Please see attached for full statement of work and schedule**

Fully scan approximately 50 (15") file boxes or approximately 200,000 pages with heavy litigation/handling for each individual file.
Each completed scan folder must be labeled individually, and each file section must be labeled and separated into sub-folders.

The forest is converting paper files into electronic versions. Each file contains six separate sections that are attached to the file folder
with two prongs. All documents must be placed back into their file and attached back onto the two prongs.

Technical Requirements / Tasks
- Files must be scanned in Black & White to at least 300 dpi and Color to at least 600 dpi as needed. End results must
have each file become a multi-page pdf file. A master directory must be created that lists all the files in
Alphabetical/numerical order. All files must be named with the file heading as found on the top right corner of each
file folder. Each file with tabs contains six different tabs that will establish six sub-folders. All sub-folders must be
labeled, and the scans inserted.
o Each file folder title will be labeled using the top right name shown on each folder
o Sub folders can all be generically labeled as follows:
Application(s)
Permit
Amendment(s)
NEPA Decision
Cultural Clearance
Correspondence
- All files will be provided in file boxes in Rochester, VT. All documents must be placed back into each file, into each
separate section, placed back onto its sections two prong holders and returned to the agency.
o Three copies of the final scans must be provided on three external hard drives. Contractor will supply the
external hard drives. It may be possible to scan directly to a shared Forest Service Pinyon folder structure.
This will be determined prior to file pick-up.
o All staples, paper clips, etc. will be removed by the contractor and discarded.
o All documents will be scanned in black and white, except color photos or color maps must be scanned in
place in color.
o Any loose sheets found in a six-section file can be scanned with the closest file tab section of that particular
file.
o Single section files can be labeled with the single name using the name on the top right of the file, but the
digital subfolders must be provided for future use.
o All completed scans must have Optical Character Recognition (ORC) and be fully searchable.
o No documents will be shredded.
o All blank back sheet images must be removed from the final scans.

Description Text:
SCOPE OF WORK:  

The objective of the project is to comply with OMB/NARA M-19-21 requirements to move to an electronic or "paperless" government. The National Forests in Mississippi seeks to digitize 116 boxes containing approximately 580,000 Lands, Minerals and Special Uses program area files and records per OMB/NARA M-19-21 requirements by December 31, 2022. This includes approximately 37 boxes with approximately 225,000 pages of associated land records containing land convenances, encroachments, acquisition, and maps. 80-85% of the documents are 8 by 11 with 20-15% being 8 by 14 or larger. Specially, 29 boxes with approximately 205,000 mineral files and projects. 50 boxes with approximately 150,000 pages of special uses authorizations. Scanning using high quality, professional scanners at a minimum of 300 DPI and digitally archiving them. The final product will be an organized digital archive of Records by site and project archived in the approved NARA Records Schedule and the ability to be stored on a government server, making it easy to search for projects, sites, or categories of records. 

REQUIREMENTS:

This project consists of the following tasks: 

The Contractor will produce a high quality, legible, and searchable digital (PDF) format of Records by saving the information to an approved electronic format that is securely and confidentially transferred from the Contractor to the Forest Service personnel. The PDF documents shall be labeled using the National Forests in Mississippi (NFMS) site or the FS filing system uses numbers, headings, and subheadings for file designations (file codes). Each site shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate program name. Each report shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate project number.

Contractor will provide electronic equipment for records storage purposes that can be used between the Contractor and the NFMS USFS personnel. The equipment must be safe and secure due to the confidential nature of these records.

If Contractor is unable to perform the specialized procedures required to scan, digitize, and archive the Records, the Contractor shall notify the Contracting Officer Representative immediately. 

Place of Performance
The Contractor shall provide technical services to scan, digitize, and archive approximately 116 boxes of Records on-site. If the Contractor cannot scan, digitize, and archive records on-site the Contractor shall properly transport, handle, and store original Records. The Contractor will take every precaution to protect the confidentiality of the Records while in transit, while at their facility, and in the process of delivering the final product.

Special Qualifications
The NFMS requires a local contractor as the NFMS reserves the right to gain access to any files the contractor is scanning if an emergency develops and land, minerals, and special uses resources are at risk (i.e., during a wildfire incident). 

DELIVERABLES:

The Contractor shall provide final products consisting of professionally digitized, of publishable quality, and with the ability to be searched and shared across platforms. Photographs and drawings must be clear. The original Records must be returned to the Agency unless the Contractor is otherwise instructed. 

The Contractor will not make available to the public or in a public format while in the services of the contractor locational data of lands, minerals and special uses resources documented by the Records.


Electronic Deliverables 
The final product will be an organized digital archive of scanned, OCR'd/searchable PDF documents labeled using the NFMS site or the FS filing system uses numbers, headings, and subheadings for file designations (file codes), stored on an external hard drive or other safely secured equipment that is portable. Each site shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate site number. Each report shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate project number. If the vendor has another viable source that will safely store the information and can transfer to the NFMS USFS personnel while maintaining security of confidential information without using a portable device, this would be preferable. 

Any new original digital materials generated while completing this project will be submitted to the NFMS. 

Quality Control 
The contractor shall develop and maintain an effective quality control program to ensure services are performed in accordance with this Statement of Work. The contractor shall develop and implement procedures to identify, prevent, and ensure non-recurrence of defective services. The contractor's quality control program is how the contractor assures the work complies with the requirement of the contract. All services shall be in accordance with accepted professional standards.

Return of Hard Copy Files
The Contractor will return five boxes of hard-copy records converted into the electronic deliverables at a time until the project is finished. Once the scanning of the five boxes is completed, all paper records will be repackaged into their original folders and returned to the NFMS supervisors office personnel at such time that a new batch is obtained. The original SF135 Records Transmittal and Receipt should be verified by the Contractor to ensure that all documents received are returned with a copy of the original SF135 Records Transmittal and Receipt is included within the returned box.

The Contractor will coordinate with the USFS personnel to transport safely and securely the Records boxes to the facility for scanning and digitizing. 

Extensions 
If it is not possible to complete the terms of this Scope of Work within the allotted timeframe, the Contractor may request, in writing, an extension from the US Forest Service. Reasonable extensions of time may be granted when it is in the best interest of the United States Government. 

OTHER PROVISIONS: 

Ownership of Data and Materials 
All original paper records, notes, photographs, maps, and reports provided to the contractor under this contract remain the property of the US Forest Service and must be returned to the NFMS upon completion of the project. All electronic records, files, and reports generated under this contract remain the property of the US Forest Service. These shall be provided to the Forest Service upon request. No information obtained under this contract shall be disseminated without written permission of the NFMS. 

Dissemination of Data 
The use of the Forest Service name, logo or likeness requires written approval from the US Forest Service and is necessary for any data presentation. The content of the presentation material must be reviewed by the Forest Service. All requests for data or information shall be referred to the Forest Service without action by the Contractor. The Forest Service must be recognized in any such presentation, and a copy of report, poster, paper, or PowerPoint presentation will be furnished to the Forest Service, upon request. 

Publications 
The Contractor shall be aware that final versions of any documents are considered property of the Government. Since all data generated by this task order is considered Government property, the Contractor is not permitted to publish the results of this project during performance of this task order without prior written approval from the Government. 

Security 
The Contractor shall continually provide for the secure safekeeping of all materials generated by this contract while those materials are in his custody. The secure, safe keeping of Lands, Minerals and Special Uses records and photos is the responsibility of the Contractor while the documents are in his possession or under his control. 

INSPECTION AND PAYMENT:

Work performed under this contract will be subject to inspection by the Contracting Officer's Representative (COR) and the LMU Program Manager and any sub-standard performance will be brought to the attention of the Contractor. 

Contracting Officer Representative (COR):  
Tyson Olinger will serve as the COR. The COR monitors all technical aspects of the contract and assists in contract administration. The COR is authorized to perform the following functions: assure that the Contractor performs the technical requirements of the contract: perform inspections necessary in connection with contract performance: maintain written and oral communications with the Contractor concerning technical aspects of the contract: issue written interpretations of technical requirements, including Government drawings, designs, specifications: monitor Contractor's performance and notifies both the Contracting Officer and Contractor of any deficiencies; coordinate availability of government furnished property. A letter of designation issued to the COR, a copy of which is sent to the Contractor, states the responsibilities and limitations of the COR, especially regarding changes in cost or price, estimates or changes in delivery dates. The COR is not authorized to change any of the terms and conditions of the resulting order.

INSPECTION PROCEDURES.

a.	The COR will monitor performance in accordance with the schedule and will ensure all services required by the order/contract are successfully performed.  All inspections will be documented and include as a minimum date and time, who performed the inspection, what was inspected, to what standard and the results.   Successful or exceptional performance will be documented as well as deficiencies in Contractor performance.   Documentation will be maintained for future reference, audit, and proof of inspection.  Any uncorrected unacceptable observation will result in an unacceptable rating, a corrective action request to the Contractor and notification to the Contracting Officer.

b.	The COR will notify the Contractor, in person, each time an unacceptable observation has been recorded and ask the Contractor to correct the problem. The COR will record the contract requirement, the specific deficiency to the requirement, the date and time it was discovered, and have the Contractor initial the entry. The Contractor will be given two working days after notification to correct the deficiency in accordance with requirements of the contract.  Deficiencies that cannot be corrected within two working days will be reported to the Contracting Officer.   The Contracting Officer will notify the Contractor and request corrective action.  

c.	The COR will re-inspect services that were found deficient to ensure corrections were made within the timeframe outlined in the contract or other agreed upon time.  The COR will not consider the services complete until all deficiencies have been corrected.  Contractor's failure to complete corrective actions will be reported immediately to the Contracting Officer for further action.

d.	The COR will accept the services provided and authorize payment upon satisfactory completion of the work. 

Quality Assurance  
The LMU Program Manager or appointee will evaluate the contractor's performance and progress under this contract on a weekly basis. 

MATERIALS AND WORKMANSHIP:

Unless otherwise provided in the contract statement of work, the contractor will furnish all materials and equipment necessary for satisfactory contract performance. When not specifically identified in the specifications, such materials and equipment shall be of the most suitable type and grade for the purpose. 

CONTRACT TERMS:   

This will be a firm fix price contract/purchase order with payment made after work has been completed and accepted via IPP.gov.

Description Text:
It is Application Development's objective that any contract following this RFI will provide US Forest Service and the eMNEPA program agile development capabilities, through introduction of new technologies and modernization of existing technologies and applications in support of the eMNEPA program mission. The objective of the eMNEPA Modernization is to conduct an evaluation of options, a proposed product roadmap, provide recommended technical solution(s), and deliver a modernized Electronic Land Management System (ELMS) in an agile manner, through collaboration with business, enterprise, application, security, and infrastructure architects.
The modernized system must empower its users with these updated qualities:
-	Holistic, mission-driven support to better transition actual land conditions to desired conditions
-	Transparent access to connected information that enables better public and partner collaboration
-	Production of land management decisions that are data-driven and knowledge-oriented
-	System features that seamlessly adapt land management lifecycles to new or different situations
-	Automated alignment of environmental analysis with law, regulation, and policy
-	Creation of intelligent reuse of historical land management data locked in documents
-	Design of smart systems that always prioritize maximum usability for system users

The goal is to produce a system that increases reuse of information, is user friendly, serves customers, automates rote processes, reduces costs, provides standardization and consistent business results.
