Solicitation Title: Utilities Invoice Processing Services for approximately 4,000 plus federal facilities/offices located across the the United States and its territories: 3,200 vendors and 15,900 utility accounts equating to approximately 205,380 utility invoices per year
Description Text:
UPDATE 5/02/2022: The Q&A has been uploaded/attached to this RFI as a PDF document for viewing and consideration.

UPDATE 4/28/2022: Please Note that Q&A Closes 4/28/2022 at 11:00 pm EST and that Q&A Response will be uploaded/attached to this RFI NLT 5/04/2022 at 12:00 pm EST. 

This initial Request for Information and Sources Sought seeks capable, proven, and established companies that provide the following services to current commercial or government customers.  Please provide relevent, current, and past performance along with cababilities statements that include business size, platforms, systems, and software utilzed.  Please submit any questions in writing NLT 4/28/2022 at 11:00 pm EST.  Q&A Responses will be published and provided as an attachment to this RFI NLT 5/04/2022 at 12:00 pm EST.  Estimated Solicitation date: June 2022.  Estimated Award Date August 2022.  Currently, this requirement is "Unrestricted" and is open to Large Business to respond based on preliminary market research.  Anticipated/Estimated maximum cost per invoice processed is $6.00 at an approximate 50% mix of manual vs. automated processing.   

1.0 BACKGROUND AND PURPOSE

Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO) is currently responsible for processing and payment of utility invoices for Twelve (12) United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) agencies, termed ‘USDA’. Utilities used by USDA and its’ agencies include Potable water, Non-potable heated and chilled water, electricity (including Demand/Reactive and Renewable electricity), natural gas, Ethanol 85 (E85), propane (LPG), steam, #1 and #2 fuel oil, diesel, bio-diesel, kerosene, coal, biomass (wood), sewer, gasoline, bulk gases (O2, N2, and CO2), recycling, and trash collection services when combined with other utilities that are not separable.

The Department requires an integrated invoice processing and payment solution that has the capability to capture cost and consumption data at the meter level (multiple meters could be included on a single invoice).  The current invoice processing system contains data for approximately 3,200 vendors and 15,900 utility accounts equating to approximately 205,380 utility invoices per year.

Currently, the utilities invoice processing and telecom invoice processing are separated by two separated contracts.  The Forest Service would like to combine these services if possible.

2.0 OBJECTIVE

The Department and its’ Agencies require an integrated invoice processing and payment solution to support respective utility vendors and to capture and report energy and water consumption from the processed invoices.

The reporting of cost and consumption of utilities and water is required of Federal Agencies by Rules, Statutes, and Executive Orders including The Energy Policy Act of 2005, and the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007. Federal Agencies must have accurate cost and consumption data by location and ZIP code for the Annual Energy Data Report, the Department of Energy (DOE) Compliance Tracking System, and the Corporate Property Automated Information System, among other mandatory reports, as well as internal tracking and decision making.

The government’s objective is to award an Indefinite Delivery-Indefinite Quality (IDIQ) contract award based on best value.

Contractor shall respond to this Statement of Objectives (SOO) by providing a Performance Work Statement (PWS), Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan (QASP), and relevant Past Performance. 

3.0 SCOPE

The Forest Service may select a contractor for utility accounts services and another contractor for telecommunications accounts services or may select a single contractor for both utility accounts services and telecommunications services, if in the best interest of the government. The Contractor shall function as the invoice processing and payment vendor for all applicable Forest Service utility accounts (and up to 12 USDA agencies) serve as the primary point of contact with these utility vendors and capture and report on energy and water consumption and conservation on all utility accounts to meet Federal Energy Acts, laws, and policies. 

The contractor will provide services to the US Forest Service and potentially other USDA agencies.

The selected contractor shall provide all necessary resources (management, supervisor, labor, tools, materials, and other items and services) to perform this scope of work.

The contractor will be required to process all invoices on time and accurately. Failure to do so will result in late fees and possible service disruptions for the Forest Service facilities. Utilities provided include natural gas, propane, electricity to include demand/reactive and renewable electricity, sewage, trash (in some but not all locations), recycling, water, purchased steam, chilled water, fuel oil, biomass, gasoline, E85, and bulk gases (O2, N2, and CO2) . This may also include Telecommunications provided to include but not limited to: wireless, landline, internet, cable, security alarms, radios, and satellite services.  A large percentage of the invoices currently processed are received as paper bills via the US Postal Service and will require some degree of manual input and/or scanning. Ideally, where possible, the contractor should maximize online bill paying to better streamline the process and for paper invoices, newer digital scanning technologies that utilize machine learning, AI, and/or automation. The contractor will need to provide access to an online tool to allow government budget, procurement and contracting personnel to view transactional data in near real time. The contractor also will be responsible for providing monthly consumption reports.

Request for Information for Collection Maintenance Services

This is a Request for Information issued by the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) for informational and planning purposes only. This is NOT a solicitation, nor is it an announcement for a forthcoming solicitation. Submission of any information in response to this notification is purely voluntary. The Government assumes no financial responsibility for any costs incurred in providing a response. This notice does not constitute a Request for Bid, Request for Quote or Request for Proposal, nor does it restrict the Government as to the ultimate acquisition approach. Responses to this notification will help to determine the acquisition strategy for any future solicitation. 
The information from this notice will help the United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA), National Agricultural Library (NAL) plan their acquisition strategy.

NAL is seeking a contractor to perform the following:

Background
NAL, part of the Agricultural Research Service (ARS) of the USDA, is one of four national libraries of the United States. The NAL collection consists of over 3 million physical items (stored in the fourteen-story Abraham Lincoln Building in Beltsville, MD) as well as extensive electronic collections. The NAL collection is a unique and valuable resource, and the centerpiece for all the services NAL provides. As a national library, NAL has a twofold mission regarding its collection: to provide access for current users and to preserve the collection for the use of future generations.

Purpose and Scope
NAL requires a contractor to provide maintenance support for the physical collection and document delivery service for NAL users.  The goal of this contract is to maintain the NAL collection in the best possible physical condition, to provide customers with quick and easy access to materials from the NAL collection, and to provide USDA employees with documents needed to support their work. The Contractor shall provide collection maintenance, document delivery and associated services as specified in the Statement of Work (SOW).

See Attached Statement of Work

Solicitation Title: IBM Mainframe Infrastructure as a Service (MIaaS)
Description Text: THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY.  This Mainframe Infrastructure as a Service (MIaaS) for IBM z Series CPU complexes and related storage systems, Direct Access Storage Devices and Virtual Tape Systems
Documents AI Summary:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is seeking to acquire Mainframe Infrastructure as a Service (MIaaS) for IBM z Series CPU complexes and related storage systems, Direct Access Storage Devices, and Virtual Tape Systems. The purpose of this Request for Information (RFI) is to gather market research and identify interested parties and potential vendors who can provide MIaaS for USDA DISC. The USDA currently operates two Enterprise Data Centers located in Kansas City and St. Louis, Missouri, with the St. Louis facility being moved to Chicago, IL. The goal of the MIaaS model is to provide a cost-effective solution that eliminates the need for capital funding, allows for easy scalability, and enables technology refreshes within the term of the agreement. The Contractor will be responsible for providing hardware, firmware, design capabilities, logistical support, field maintenance, and systems engineering.

The scope of work includes performing a Technical Delivery Assessment, generating and installing an Input/Output Configuration Program (IOCP), customizing the support element or Hardware Management Console, ensuring hardware toleration maintenance software fixes are applied and tested, providing education and support for system programmers on new features, and maintaining the systems and providing technology refreshment for the term of the contract. The Contractor will also be required to deliver a Contractor Project Plan (CPP) that outlines their approach, timeline, tools, and coordination and reporting processes. Meetings will be held with the Government to discuss maintenance issues, performance reviews, administrative matters, and project startup.

The Contractor will install IBM manufactured CPU complex equipment on premise via a Capacity Services Model solution. The solution must integrate with existing IBM mainframes and allow for capacity adjustments based on DISC's changing customer base and usage levels. The Contractor must provide OEM qualified hardware maintenance parts, service, and labor using only new equipment and parts. Gray Market Goods or Counterfeit Electronic Parts are strictly prohibited.

Interested parties are requested to submit their responses to the RFI, including company information, existing federal contracts, any concerns with the Performance Work Statement, experience with the federal government, industry standard contract duration, and any alternative brand names that can fulfill the requirements. The deadline for submission is Wednesday, April 27, 2022.

Documents AI Summary:
Background: The solicitation is for a project titled 'Forest Service Heritage Records Scanning' for the Kaibab National Forest. The objective of the project is to preserve historic records pertaining to the Heritage Resources Program by digitizing all records as per Executive Order 13589 of November 9, 2011, as well as the Federal Records Act amendments of 2014. The project involves scanning 25 boxes containing records associated with archaeological projects, historic Forest Service administrative projects, and other associated projects using high-quality, professional scanners at a minimum of 300 DPI and digitally archiving them. The final product will be an organized digital archive of Records by site and project archived in the approved NARA Records Schedule and the ability to be stored on a government server, making it easy to search for projects, sites, or categories of records.
Work Details: The Contractor is required to produce a high quality, legible, and searchable digital (PDF) format of Records by saving the information to an approved electronic format that is securely and confidentially transferred from the Contractor to the Forest Service personnel. The PDF documents shall be labeled using the Kaibab National Forest (KNF) site or project numbering system. Each site shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate site number. Each report shall be its own individual electronic document named with the appropriate project number. The Contractor will provide electronic equipment for records storage purposes that can be used between the Contractor and the Kaibab USFS personnel. If Contractor is unable to perform the specialized procedures required to scan, digitize, and archive the Records, they shall notify the Representative immediately. The Contractor shall provide technical services to prepare the approximately 25 boxes of Records to be scanned, digitized, and archived. They shall properly transport, handle, and store original Records taking every precaution to protect their confidentiality while in transit, at their facility, and in delivering the final product.
Period of Performance: The period of performance for this contract is from April 13th, 2022 to April 29th, 2022.

Solicitation Title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source - Google Cloud Platform -- Google Earth Engine
Description Text:
Description:

The Google Cloud Platform (GCP) provides NASS the opportunity to build early season predictive Cropland Data Layer’s (P-CDL’s) which is a FY22 NASS IMAGES project top ten agency priority project, as well as research crop emergence, analyze and host Planet high-resolution satellite imagery, assess agriculture disasters, research land cover classifiers, and build Cropland Data Layers (CDL) for purposes of acreage estimation.  

Additionally, USDA/FAS will be providing NASS 2022 Planet collections and NASS will use the GCP to store and analyze the Planet data from the prior season collections of 2021 and upcoming 2022 collections.  The Planet data collections are expected to exceed 65 Terabytes within the GCP contract provision of 100 Terabytes.  The Planet collection ingress into GCP will assist with agricultural monitoring of acreage, yield, crop condition and assessment for the 2022 growing season.    

The P-CDL layers are derived by NASS for research purposes to predict prospective plantings for the IMAGES program, a NASS top 10 initiative.  No other geospatial applications can perform these functions and features provided by GCP with the accuracy, timeliness, and functionality required to produce both the CDL and P-CDL products.  The required functionality includes processing of satellite imagery and ground reference datasets to perform supervised image classifications (i.e., CDL & P-CDL) for purposes of deriving acreage estimates.

Period of Performance:  12 Months from Date of Award

Current market research indicates that NT Concepts (Incumbent) is the sole provider of Google Cloud Platform licenses. NT Concepts is the sole authorized US reseller, GSA Schedule holder, distributer, and VAR of these products. Additionally, to date, no other known geospatial or imagery cloud provider delivers, curates, and provides free and open access to Earth observation collections from Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, Landsat 7, 8, and 9 satellites.  This benefits NASS to alleviate downloading each of these satellites’ collections over the continental US and puts the imagery adjacent to the data, removing burdensome continental imagery downloads to the Agency; therefore, the following circumstance justifies limiting the source under FAR 6.304 Open Market Acquisitions, FAR 8.405-6  Federal Supply Schedules, and FAR 13.106-3 Simplified Acquisition Procedures: Only one source is capable of providing the supplies or services required at the level of quality required.

Anticipated Date of Award: ASAP

NOTE: Vendor must be an authorized reseller of the specific Google Cloud Platforms - Google Earth Engine.

Submission Instructions: Vendors are invited to submit a response to this notice of intent to sole source notification by 5:00 PM EST on February 25, 2022, to the attention of Mr. David Tibbs, Contract Specialist, david.tibbs@usda.gov.  All questions asked shall be submitted in writing to the Primary Point of Contract and will be addressed accordingly.

Responses should be no more than 10 pages and include the following information:


	Organization Name;
	Contact Name, e-mail address and phone number;
	Organizations DUNS number;
	Business size status for the applicable NAICS code;
	A statement of capability to perform details of the requirement;
	Confirmation of authorized reseller status for applicable Google Cloud Platform – Google Earth Engine.


Interested vendors are instructed to provide only the data requested. No proprietary classified confidential or sensitive information should be included in your response. The Government reserves the right to use any nonproprietary technical information in any resultant solicitation(s). Any additional information will not be reviewed by the USDA.

Description Text:
The Agricultural Research Service (ARS), Office of the Chief Information Office (OCIO), the U.S. Department of Agriculture in Beltsville, MD has a requirement which is described in the attached Statement of Work for:

Microsoft Azure Government Cloud Hosting

Period of Performance:  
Base Period:	        03.30.2022 06.29.2022
Option Period I:	06.30.2022 09.29.2022
Option Period II:	09.30.2022 12.30.2022

Please prepare a 'Quote' and ensure that it meets all of the criteria listed.  Please include the Business Name, DUNS Number, CAGE code and 508
Compliance. Also ensure that the quote includes all shipping (F.O.B.Destination, within consignee's premise) and any other applicable charges to:
USDA/ARS/OCIO
5601 Sunnyside Avenue
Beltsville, MD  20705

Submissions are requested to be sent electronically to Marian.Dyson@usda.gov by 12 noon (or earlier) on Wednesday, February 9, 2022. 

The quote should include a firm fixed price (FFP) format for the requirement.  

The Government intends to make an award based on the following:
Factor 1:  Technical Capability
Technical capability shall be rated as either "Technically Acceptable" or "Technically Unacceptable." To be eligible for award, Factor 1 must be evaluated as "Technically Acceptable".
Technical Capability Subfactors:
-	Contractor must be a Microsoft Managed Service Provider
-	Provide continued Infrastructure-as-a-Service hosting in Microsoft Azure Government Cloud based on current configurations 
-	Provide cloud environment for production, staging, and development purposes to support the complete systems lifecycle
-	Provide cloud professional support services
-	Virtual machines must be able to support Windows and Linux operating systems
-	Services must support the current ARIS and ARS Public Website environment without the need to move, re-install or re-config the current environment
-	All applications hosted must be available 7 days a week, 24 hours a day
-	Provide Azure Backup and Recovery Services including data redundancy and geo redundant storage
-	Be able to provide auto-provisioning, decommissioning, and scheduling services
-	Ability to be interoperable with existing ARS cloud and external environments
-	Ensure Azure scalability to maximize performance, usage, and costs
-	Ensure access to Azure on-line tool to allow authenticated and authorized users to manage Azure services including provisioning and de-provisioning, 
configuration, and performance monitoring
-	Provide customized on-line usage tool for monitoring usage to determine funding needs

Factor 2:  Past Performance
Past performance shall be evaluated to determine the extent to which it demonstrates experience likely to result in satisfactory performance. Past performance shall be rated as either "Pass" or "Fail." Offerors that do not possess a record of past performance that is similar in size, scope, and complexity will receive a "Neutral" rating.  The Government may consider past performance information from individual contract references and in the aggregate.  In evaluating Past Performance, the Government reserves the right to use information provided by the offeror, Past Performance Questionnaire (PPQ) and any additional information obtained from other sources known or identified by the Government.  To be eligible for award, Factor 2 must not be evaluated as "Fail."

Factor 3:  Price 
The offeror shall be evaluated to determine if the quoted prices are fair and reasonable. The Government may use any of the price analysis techniques prescribed by the FAR to evaluate the offeror's price reasonableness. Reasonableness may be established by the existence of adequate price competition. The Government may determine that a quotation is unacceptable if the option prices are significantly unbalanced.

Please direct any questions concerning this order to Marian.Dyson@usda.gov.

Solicitation Title: Intent to Sole Source-Google Earth Engine
Description Text:
Intent to Sole Source-

This is a notice of intent to award a sole source contract and is not a request for competitive proposals.

The USDA, NRCS proposes to procure Google Earth Engine Services to support NRCS’s creation of a nationwide soils information product to support conservation on a 12-month period. The subscription Enterprise Agreement for Public Sector GCP – GEE (Part No. 9A92-40AE-8D00 -2) 02 GCP – GEE Technical Support Services (Setup) exhibits the Google Earth Engine platform subscription for 10 simultaneous users and is inclusive of all costs to the Government from the platform standpoint – including storage (upload and access to stored or derived data), access, processing, and dissemination. The proposed Enterprise License for GCP-GEE allows for 10 licensed users each with unlimited use. This workload will include Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services such as Google Earth Engine (GEE), Google Cloud Storage, Google Compute Engine, Google BigQuery GIS, Google Cloud AI Platform Google, Cloud Identity limited to "Earth Observation and Remote Sensing Workloads" for up to 10 users. The Expert Technical Support Services will be provided for initialization and configuration of the platform. Expert technical support services are proposed in the amount of 56 hours of labor during the setup period.

This is on a basis other than full and open competition, Sole Source, Firm Fixed Price from Next Tier Concepts, Inc.

The proposed contract action is for commercial items for which the Government intends to solicit in accordance with (IAW) Federal Acquisitions Regulations (FAR) 13.106-1(b), Only one source reasonably available and no other supplies or services will satisfy the agency’s requirement.

The North American Industry Classification (NAICS) for this acquisition is 518210 - Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services. This is not a solicitation for competitive quotes, however, if any other interested party believes that it can provide the required products, it may submit a statement of capabilities. Capability statements must display sufficient detail and evidence to determine if the party has the ability to provide the required products.  The statement of capabilities must be furnished in writing and emailed to Sherita.glanton@va.gov by September 16, 2021 at 2:00 p.m. Central Standard Time (CST).  The government will not pay for information received in response to this announcement.

A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed requirement based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement in the future. This is a notice, and a solicitation will not be issued. Oral communications will not be accepted in response to this notice.

Description Text:
SOW RO Scanning Contract 2021
Location: 	Greater Missoula Area, Missoula, MT
	
Description of work:  This project is for scanning documents currently located in the greater Missoula Area. These documents come in multiple sizes including (but not limited to) 8.5"x11", 8.5"x14", and larger formats and number approximately 75,000 items in 30 boxes. It is estimated that approximately 80% are 8.5"x11", 15% are 8.5"x14", and 5% are larger formats. 
Standard and Legal documents will be scanned at 400 dots per inch (DPI) and larger format documents will be scanned at 600 DPI unless otherwise noted. 
All documents will be shipped from their current locations in Missoula, MT. Due to the large number of files to be reviewed prior to shipping to contractor, boxes will be shipped as made available over the length of this contract.
The naming convention for indexing these files must be easily understood for file location and retrieval. A list of file codes for use in indexing will be provided as attachment A and will be followed by project name, location, or other identifying marker. Examples: 7140_BitterrootValley, 7300_KamiahOffice, etc.
Scanned and indexed files will be stored on a hard drive and mailed to: Ryan Hemphill; 26 Fort Missoula Rd; Missoula, MT 59804. Files that have been transferred to a hard drive and shipped shall be maintained until receipt of hard drive has been acknowledged by the FS. After this point, the files will be destroyed by contractor.
Field verification: Contractor shall field verify all existing conditions of files of this contract prior to bid and/or starting work.
Submittals: 
It shall be the Contractor's responsibility to submit all items prior to beginning work. The Contractor shall allow 5-days for processing of submittals by the Government. Approvals must be obtained before starting work unless otherwise noted. Submittals are required for the following:
-	Digital Proof of 1st scan of each paper size
-	Hard Drive(s) with Scanned Files upon completion of scanning
Provided by FS: Files to be scanned.
Site Visit: A Pre-bid site visit is required and must be scheduled with COR. 
Schedule: Prior to starting work, submit a schedule showing anticipated start and completion dates for work items. Please coordinate specifics with COR a minimum of 2-weeks prior to anticipate start date to ensure individuals are available to turn over files. 
Performance Period:
The performance period for this contract is 365 days. If unforeseen disruptions to work occur (i.e. office closures, illness, etc.), coordinate start with CO and performance period will be adjusted as necessary to ensure suitable working conditions. 
Forest Service Point of Contact: Ryan Hemphill, ryan.hemphill@usda.gov, 406-329-3031

Solicitation Title: FEITH Annual Software Maintenance Bundled Maintenance
Description Text:
The National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS), Information & Technology Division (ITD), the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) in Washington, DC has a requirement which is described below for:

FEITH Systems and Software
Annual Software Maintenance Bundled Maintenance 

Period of Performance:  
Base Year:		10.01.2020 09.30.2021
Option Year I:	        10.01.2021 09.30.2022
Option Year II:	        10.01.2022 09.30.2023
Option Year III:	10.01.2023 09.30.2024
Option Year IV:	10.01.2024 09.30.2025


BRAND NAME JUSTIFICATION
National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) currently deploys FEITH Systems and Software.  This requirement is for annual software maintenance.  The costs of transitioning to a competing software go beyond the cost of the license.  Cost to the Government includes installation of the software, adoption of a new license management process, training for users, and conversion to another.  Training can have hidden costs in that it can take a great deal of time and there is a likelihood of lost productivity as users climb the learning curve for a new unfamiliar tool.  Total switching costs involved with adoption of a new software are burdensome to the government.  

Please prepare a GSA Quote and ensure that it meets all of the criteria listed.  Please include the Business Name, DUNS Number, CAGE code, and GSA Schedule Contract Number on the quote.  Also ensure that the quote includes all shipping (F.O.B. Destination, within consignee's premise) and any other applicable charges to:

USDA/NASS
1400 Independence Avenue SW
Washington, DC  20250

The quote should include a firm fixed price (FFP) format for the requirement.  The Government intends to make an award based on the technically acceptable offer with the lowest evaluated price (inclusive of all line items).

Description Text:
Data Archiving and Storage Services

CONTRACTING OFFICE
U.S. Department of Agriculture, Departmental Administration

DESCRIPTION 
This is a SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE to determine the availability and capability of businesses to provide Data Archiving & Storage Services off GSA Federal Supply Schedules. Work is per the Performance Work Statement (PWS) attached.

The scope of this contract is to provide full-service data archival storage and retrieval services; including automated receipt and intake of line data feed reports; scanning and indexing of paper documents;  and conversion of legacy microfiche and microfilm.  All digital images shall be stored in a secure, online repository viewable via a web interface 24x7x365/366 until the prescribed retention period expiration date is reached.
Contractor will be required to make deliverables as stated in the PWS.
Contractors must satisfy the requirements listed in the PWS.  Provide a detailed description of your company's (including its teammates, if applicable) experience and demonstrated abilities to deliver ALL needed services as stated in the PWS. Documentation must be provided to show the company has the capabilities and experience to complete these services. 

A Firm-Fixed Price contract is anticipated. Contract is anticipated to be an option year contract, for a base year with four (4) one-year option periods. The duration of this project is one year, 06/01/2020 through 05/31/2021 annually. Exercising option periods is up to the discretion of USDA, NFC.  The current incumbent is Iron Mountain Information Management, LLC. The NAICS Code is 518210.

Interested business that can offer this off GSA FSS are encouraged to respond to this notice.  However, be advised that generic capability statements are not sufficient for effective evaluation of respondents' capacity and capability to perform the specific work as required.  Responses must directly demonstrate the company's capability, experience, and/or ability to marshal resources to effectively and efficiently perform each of the tasks described above at a sufficient level of detail to allow definitive numerical evaluation; and evidence that the contractor can satisfy the minimum requirements listed above while in compliance with FAR 52.219-14 ("Limitations on Subcontracting").  Failure to definitively address each of these factors will result in a finding that respondent lacks capability to perform the work.

Responses to this notice shall be limited to 10 pages, and must include:
1.	Company name, mailing address, e-mail address, telephone and FAX numbers, website address (if available), and the name, telephone number, and e-mail address of a point of contact having the authority and knowledge to clarify responses with Government representatives.
2.	Name, title, telephone number, and e-mail addresses of individuals who can verify the demonstrated capabilities identified in the responses.
3.	Business size for NAICS 518210 
4.	DUNS number, CAGE Code, Tax Identification Number, and company structure (Corporation, LLC, partnership, joint venture, etc.).  Companies also must be registered in the System for Award Management (https://www.sam.gov/portal/public/SAM/) to be considered as potential sources.
5.	Identification of the firm's GSA Schedule contract(s) by Schedule number and contract number and SINs that are applicable to this potential requirement are also requested.
Teaming arrangements are acceptable, and the information required above on the company responding to this announcement, should also be provided for each entity expected to be teammates of the respondent for performance of this work.

To the maximum extent possible, please submit non-proprietary information.  Any proprietary information submitted should be identified as such and will be properly protected from disclosure.

Solicitation Title: USDA Water and Climate Information System- Data Ingestion
Description Text:
USDA Water and Climate Information System- Data Ingestion
1.1 Introduction This Sources Sought notice is being issued under the procedures of FAR Part 10 via Federal Business Opportunities. The government is requesting information for Water and Climate Information System- Data Ingestion. This request for information is open to all interested parties. The NAICS for this requirement has not been determined.
1.2 BackgroundThe United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is issuing this request for information as part of a market research and information gathering to determine the availability of technical capabilities to receive, convert, and aggregate data for storage in the Snow Program databases according to a planned architectural roadmap. USDA is seeking solutions that are cost effective, interoperable, secure (IAW FISMA requirements), reliable, robust, and scalable. At a minimum, vendors must provide a strategic vision and framework on how USDA would transform the Water & Climate Information System data ingestion to include, but not limited to, GOES satellite stations, cellular infrastructure, iridium stations (low earth orbit satellites), meteor burst stations (including: master stations, external downlink and uplink servers). All vendors are requested to provide a description of their costing model along with their response.
1.3 Request for Information QuestionsVendors must respond in written format to the following questions:
1. USDA is seeking to establish technical capabilities to receive, convert, and aggregate data for storage in the Snow Program databases. Does your firm provide these types of services and are they readily accessible via government acquisition vehicles?
2. Based on the current USDA architecture, does your firm have the capability to develop an architectural roadmap?
3. Does your firm have solution capability for central management of data ingestion? The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economics Research Service, intends to negotiate and award on a Justification for Other Than Full and Open Competition (JOFOC) basis. A purchase order will be one-year firm-fixed price (plus four option years) awarded to Oxford Economics USA, Inc. of Wayne, PA for a Macroeconomic Forecasting and Analysis of Macroeconomic Developments Affecting U.S. Agriculture monthly subscription. Anticipated Date of Award: 6/25/2019.

Several factors make Oxford Economics USA, Inc. eligible to meet ERS's Macroeconomic Forecasting and Analysis of Macroeconomic Developments Affecting U.S. Agriculture monthly subscription requirement.

a.	Fully integrated global economic model: Oxford's individual country models are fully linked through global assumptions about trade volumes and prices, competitiveness, capital flows, interest and exchange rates, and commodity prices. b.	Comprehensive country coverage: Eighty countries are examined in detail, including major developing countries important to USDA, such as China, India, and Brazil, as well as the European Union. The rest of the world economy is covered in six trading blocs.c.	Monthly updates: Oxford offers the latest economic data, macroeconomic forecasting, and Oxford's baseline forecast updates which are available monthly.d.	Windows-based PC software: Oxford's economic models come with software that allows ERS analysts to change model assumptions, add new variables and equations, and build their own economic models.

Solicitation Title: NOTICE OF INTENT TO SOLE SOURCE: DATA2BIO LLC
Description Text:
The USDA ARS - WBSC intends to award a sole source Firm Fixed Purchase Order (PO) to Data2Bio (D2B), LLC, 1111 WOI Road 2052 Roy Y Carver Co-Lab, Ames, IA 50011-1085. This order will attain purchase a Data2bio Services: DNA Extraction & GBS Analysis for lettuce samples, project management, data analysis report and delivery in support of the ongoing research at USDA ARS, Crop Improvement & Protection Research Unit (CIPRU), Salinas, CA.
The Firm Fixed PO will include the following:
1. DATA2BIO SERVICES: DNA EXTRACTION & GBS ANALYSIS OF CUSTOMER-SUPPLIED FREEZE-DRIED TISSUE SAMPLES, WHICH INCLUDES:
* Conduct 2-BP TBGS® genotyping-by-sequencing analysis of 524 diversity panel samples (wild varieties and established cultivars) of lettuce (2.5 gb)
* Data Analysis:-- QC & trimming-- Alignment to reference genome-- SNP calls/SNP-typing of each line
2. DATA2BIO PROJECT MANAGEMENT, DATA ANALYSIS REPORTS & DELIVERY
USDA ARS, CIPRU research has an essential requirement to maintain the continuity of their ongoing research & analyses for genotyping their lettuce samples. As a result, Data2Bio (D2B) previously perform genotyping on 600 lettuce cultivars provided by the Location; and now the Location research requires to have "tunable Genotyping-By-Sequencing" (tGBS) analysis performed on 524 lettuce varieties for studies of abiotic stress tolerance. The tGBS service includes DNA extraction, genotyping using proprietary tGBS approach, and preliminary data analysis. tGBS analysis is a proprietary genotyping developed and used only by D2B.
D2B developed an improved genotyping approach that is based on a larger number of reads (tGBS). Hence, the method produces much smaller error rate, less missing data across lines, high confidence of SNP calls, and what is very important for our analysis, it can confidently genotype heterozygous loci. This method also yields data that are much easier to analyze and do not require computers for big data analysis. Compared to conventional GBS analysis tGBS is more stringent leading to more reproducible results. Also, the final analysis of data is easier, faster, and cheaper than with the conventional GBS analysis, leading to substantial savings (details are in Plant Genome, 2015, vol. 8, p. 1-10). Most importantly, tGBS can identify heterozygous loci, while GBS cannot.
Pursuant to the authority of 41 U.S.C. 253(c)(1) and FAR 6.302-1, Only One Responsible Source and No Other Supplies or Services Will Satisfy Agency Requirements.
The award date will be on or about December 18, 2018; and this Notice of Intent is not considered as a Request for Quotation (RFQ) nor is it a Request for Proposals (RFP). No solicitation document is available and telephone requests will not be honored. Interested persons may identify their interest and capability to respond to this requirement. A determination, by the Government, to compete this proposed PO based on responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. The Government will consider all responses received by 09:30AM PST, December 17, 2018. Information received will be considered solely for determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. Inquiries will only be accepted via email to the Primary Point of Contact: Washingtoni.NLN@ars.usda.gov.

Solicitation Title: Physical Infrastructure Program Management and Collaboration Solution
Description Text:
Synopsis for Physical Infrastructure Program Management and Collaboration Solution as a Service
The Forest Service is seeking to acquire a cloud-based, commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) infrastructure capital program and project management information system (the system) to support the agency's recovery efforts following the 2017 hurricane season. The system will be used by Forest Service and other Federal agencies, cooperating state and local agencies, non-governmental organizations, consultants and their subcontracted teams, and constructors and their subcontracted teams. The system will used by the Forest Service to manage the comprehensive program of projects as the authoritative source of information used by all members of each project team. The system will improve project transparency and accountability and reduce the administrative burden on project teams. The system will support the entire project life cycle from planning and approval, design, procurement, construction, and handover to operations.
The Forest Service's business analysis and market research has indicated that there are several COTS cloud solutions with the potential to meet the agency's requirements. Because of the urgent timeline needed to implement a solution before major architect-engineer, construction, and design-build contracts are awarded, the Forest Service will only consider systems that are currently running in the Amazon Gov. Cloud (AWS.gov). Solutions that are currently running in Amazon East/West but can run in the AWS.gov that can meet all requirements by August 17, 2018; will also be considered.
Offerors seeking to submit a proposal are required to submit a non-binding statement of intent to propose by 2PM Eastern on July 30, 2018. This statement shall not exceed one page in length and shall provide the name of the solution to be proposed and links to information about the system, two points of contact, DUNS number, current registration status in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov), and a ranked list of top three times to host a two-hour technical implementation interactive webinar to be held on August 1-2, 2018 with Forest Service information technology staff. Offerors are responsible for providing the webinar environment. The following times are available for the webinar each day: 0930-1130 Eastern; 1300-1500 Eastern; 1530-1730 Eastern. If necessary, additional times will be made available on August 3, 2018. Offerors will be granted their preferred times by the receipt order of their statements of intent.
This procurement is set-aside for small businesses. Teaming Arrangements and Joint Ventures will be considered for award. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) codes associated with this requirement are: 518210 Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services. The magnitude of this solicitation is between $100,000 and $250,000.00. The contract duration is three years from the date of award with unpriced options for an additional two years in three month increments to allow for program completion and closeout activities.
Award determination will be best value using tradeoffs. When determining best value, past performance followed by technical capability and experience is more important than price. Past performance, technical capability and experience when combined are more important than price.
Key system features include:1. External and Internal collaboration tool that can be used by both internal Forest Service employees and external customers and vendors. 2. Ability to track documents and correspondence with a history function or log.3. Master level scheduling that allows a broad overview of project progress. 4. Mobile component for instantaneous communication and ability to make updates and take pictures on job sites using phones or tablets. Mobile apps need to include offline capacity and syncing when the device is near a signal again.5. Centralized workflow and unlimited file capacity for the documents associated with the workflow.6. Access to the application is based on role with fine grain permissions as well as separate log-in credentials for each user.7. Robust and customizable reporting features.8. Application will provide for management of user level access control to be administered by FS project leadership.9. All data sets created, stored or manipulated by the solution design will be readily available for long term electronic storage and back up as part of official records requirements for construction projects.

Description Text:
JUSTIFICATION & APPROVAL FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITIONSOLE SOURCE
REQUIREMENT: Process and Add Temporary Hiring Data to Forest Service (FS) Network Hiring Site
1.  CONTRACTING ORGANIZATION:  Coronado National Forest (CNF), Forest Service, United States Department of Agriculture (USDA).
2.  DESCRIPTION OF ACTION:  Sole source acquisition for the procurement of the processing and addition of hiring data into the FS's network internal hiring site to be performed by NuTandem.
3.  DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES:  The vendor is required to import and convert approximately 25,000 rows of raw data to build a site that can be queried, sorted, and filtered to store data about applicants, including: personal information, announcements, occupations, locations applied to, positions selected for hiring, reference information, etc.
4.  AUTHORITY:  41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1) and Federal Acquisition Regulation 6.302-1, Only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.
5.  APPLICABILITY OF AUTHORITY:  Access to the FS computer network is required in order to transmit data and complete work within the FS hosted site.
NuTandem has a current contract with the FS Region 3 (R3) Office of Communications and Engagement.  Because of this contract, NuTandem already has access to the FS intranet. For a new vendor to obtain access to this site would take up to 10 weeks. This work must be completed by March 9, 2018 in order to complete the selections phase of the centralized temporary hiring process.
Not having this service completed by these dates would cause delays in the onboarding of 175 temporary employees. Therefore delaying field work in the areas of recreation, biology, engineering, archeology, and more.  This would affect service to the public in Arizona, New Mexico, Texas, and Oklahoma.
Similar work has been performed by FS employees; however, FS employees are not experts in this system. It would likely take 2-3 months of training for an employee to understand the system to the extent that this project could be completed.
6.  EFFORTS TO OBTAIN COMPETITON:  The R3 Office of Communication and Engagement was contacted to see if there were any other vendors who already have access to the FS intranet and email systems. The R3 Office of Communication and Engagement stated that NuTandem was the only vendor known that has current FS credentials and access in order to complete the work within the required timeframes.
7.  PRICE FAIR AND REASONABLE:  Online searches found the estimated price per hour for a computer coding/data processing technician to be between $75.00-$150.00 per hour. The cost per hour of this requirement is within these values.
8.  MARKET RESEARCH:  Market research for this requirement included online searches, discussions with FS subject matter experts, an SBA Dynamic Small Business search, and calls to vendors as outlined in sections of this justification and approval.
9.  ANY OTHER FACTS SUPPORTING THE USE OF OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION:  The FS hiring site was created and coded by NuTandem during fiscal year 2017 through an existing contract with R3 Office of Communication and Engagement. Having built the site, NuTandem has thorough knowledge of the intricacies of the system and required data formatting, as well as the requirements for the end user interface for the website
10.  INTERESTED SOURCES:  An SBA Dynamic Small Business search under NAICS code 518210 with the keyword "data" found 35 small businesses in Arizona performing similar services. Three vendors were contacted for input on this type of requirement. Two companies stated they did not perform this specific type of work and could not provide information. One company stated they do perform this type of work and that they have seen cases in past where gaining access to a government network can take a significant amount of time.
NuTandem is the only known vendor who has current credentialing and access to the FS network in order to complete the work within the required timeframe.
11.  STEPS TO FOSTER COMPETITION:  The CNF contracting office thoroughly investigates each procurement request to ensure maximum competition.  If this requirement should arise again in the future, market research will again be performed to see if additional organizations have entered this marketplace.
12.  CONTRACTING OFFICER'S CERTIFICATION:  I hereby certify that the above justification is accurate and complete to the best of my knowledge and belief.//Signed//Joleen DedmonContracting Officer

The USDA, Rural Development (RD is seeking sources for Market Research ONLY to support the Real Estate Tax support services on a large portfolio of both escrowed and non-escrowed direct loans listed below.The applicable NAICS code is 518210 Data Processing, Hosting and Related Services.
Interested parties sought for non personal services contract for Real Estate Tax Services Support and related Services requersted by the government. Project will be solicited as an Invitation For Bids (IFB) contract to include all sections in the Sacramento Real Estate Division, Corp of Engineers.
NO AWARD will be made from this Sources Sought. NO SOLICTATION, SPECIFICATION, OR DRAWINGS ARE AVAILABLE AT THIS TIME.Potential offerors having the skills and capabilities necessary to perform the described services below are invited to provide feedback via email to:
All responses will be analyzed in order to determine the appropriate strategy for a potential future acquisition. Particularly, the purpose of this NOTICE is to gain knowledge of potential small business sources including certified HUBZone small business, Service-Disabled Veteran Owned small business, Veteran-Owned small business, Certified 8(a) small business, Women-Owned small business, etc. Other than Small Business are encouraged to respond to this notice as it may be that the market will not indicate significant Small Business entities able to perform service type of this magnitude..
Project Description: This is a non-personal services contract to provide real-estate tax support service on a large portfolio of escrowed and non-escrowed real estate loans, Comply with Public Law 100-242, establish and maintain escrow account for real estate taxes, tracking and maintaining status of all real estate tax liability and payments in RD's loan portfolio which encompasses properties throughout the United States with large numbers in remote rural areas, familiarity with loans and real estate taxing authorities in rural farming communities, ability of obtaining and reporting tax information and bills in an automated format, providing check printing service when requirements are made by taxing jurisdiction for a cashier's check or certified funds, and experience with using and formatting data in our loan servicing software FiServ/LoanServ. Also, please provide information related to GSA GWAC vehicles through which these services might be solicited.
Anticipated solicitation issuance date is on or about January 15, 2017 and the estimated proposal due date is on or about February 15, 2017. The official synopsis citing the solicitation number will be issued on Federal Business Opportunities (www.fbo.gov) and inviting firms to register electronically to receive a copy of the solicitation when it is issued.
CAPABILITIES AND QUALIFICATIONS:
a. Qualified personnel are required with recent knowledge and experience in:
1. Interested parties must be experts in Real Estate Tax Servicing of a large portfolio of loans, knowledge and experience with rural taxing jurisdiction across all of the United States, familiarity working with Fiserv/LoanServ software. Do they have a good working relationship with taxing jurisdictions across all of the United States? Do they have experience in handling possible real estate taxation problems as they arise? Do they have nationwide experience working with government agencies?
2. Period of Performance: The period of performance shall be for one (1) Base Year of 12 months and four (4) 12-month option years. Period of Performance begins as soon as contract is executed in 2017.
3. Offeror's type of business and Business Size (Other than Small Business, Small Business, Certified 8a, Certified HUB Zone, and Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB), etc.).
4. Firm's capability to perform a contract of this magnitude and complexity based on the scope of work (include the firm's capability to execute real estate tax services and escrow support services in the last (3) years): provide a brief description of the project(s), customer name, timeliness of performance, customer satisfaction, and dollar value of the fees charged. Provide at least three (3) examples maximum three (3) pages.
RESULTS INFORMATION: Respondents will not be notified of the results of the evaluation. Firms responding to this sources sought announcement, who fail to provide ALL of the required information requested, will not be used to help the Government make the acquisition decision, which is the intent of this sources sought announcement.
Your response is limited to 10 single-sided pages total. Sources Sought market research for "Real Estate Tax Support Services."
All interested contractors or potential offerors having the skills and capabilities necessary to perform the described services above should notify this office in writing by email or mail by 1:00 PM on Januray 2, 2015. Submit response and information to:

Description Text:
THIS NOTICE OF INTENT IS NOT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS. This notice is for informational purposes only and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government. In accordance with FAR 6.302-1 and 41 USC 25(c)(1), the service needed by the USDA is available from only one responsible source and no other type of service will satisfy the needs of the USDA. This notice is not a request for competitive quotations. No solicitation document is available and telephone requests will not be honored. Potential sources who believe they can provide the services listed are invited to submit substantiating documentation in writing to the identified point of contact.Information received will be considered solely for the purposes of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. Oral communications are not acceptable in response to this notice. A determination by the Government to compete this proposed contract based on responses to this notice is solely at the discretion of the Government. The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economic Research Service (ERS), intends to award a sole source contract to Agra Europe for global coverage of food and agricultural policies, trade, WTO issues, data, and summaries of analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade. 1.0 Introduction
ERS plays a strategic role in developing the USDA Baseline doing research and analysis of the implications to U.S. agriculture and food security of shocks both in the United States and around the world. In undertaking such a role, the analysts at ERS need to draw on a variety of data from outside of the USDA. One such tool required is a comprehensive system of economic and financial data, such as the Agra Europe system. Such a tool, which can be used on a desktop computer, facilitates doing a variety of analyses. ERS does not have the internal resources to develop and maintain such a data system. It is therefore necessary that we purchase such from an outside vendor.
2.0 Background
ERS publishes monthly and quarterly reports containing consistent U.S. and international nonagricultural data with a data history from the inception of the data source. The monthly forecast and reports about history require documentation to support ongoing reports and staff analysis done for the Secretary of Agriculture and other policymakers. The data needs to be developed on a basis consistent with the source entity to assure reliability in performing complex analyses of policy decisions, as well as reflect the interdependence of individual countries in the global market in Windows-based PC software such as Microsoft Office. The organization providing this has to have at least a 25 year track record
3.0 Scope
This Statement of Work covers the provision of data from July 24, 2016 - July 23, 2017.
The agency's main objectives for this service is to improve its function in providing the USDA with analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade from a European perspective. In depth information and analysis of European agriculture are available daily, covering commodities, policies, and topical issues such as the environment, food safety, ecology, biofuels, climate change, animal disease and welfare, water shortages, weather , and other topics of interest. For these activities, we need:1) A Windows-compatible global database system that is usable in generating tables, graphs, and spreadsheets. The database system needs to be installable on standard PCs, and the ERS LAN with the ability for users to look at data both graphically and in spreadsheet format to be manipulated by users. In addition, the data should be well documented.
2) The database needs to be available within an hour of release by the source entity to ERS and electronically via the internet including the updated historical data set used in outlook and research. This data set needs to be updated as new data become available.
3) Training, documentation, and technical support for these products and services. This includes up to 2 training sessions at ERS's discretion.
4.0 Required Data
Agra Europe provides comprehensive global coverage of food and agricultural policies, trade, WTO issues, data, and summaries of analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade-from a European perspective. In depth information and analysis of European agriculture are available daily, covering commodities, policies, and topical issues such as the environment, food safety, ecology, biofuels, climate change, animal disease and welfare, water shortages, weather, and other topics of interest.
Examples of research where Agra Europe was used and was the only source of information available include the following:
• Agra Europe is an online intelligence service for the European Union's Common Agricultural Policy, as well as providing expert coverage of global agricultural policy, legislation and trade in farm commodities. Agra Europe is a well-established source of information, providing ERS staff with information to assist in informing agriculture professionals, officials, and policy makers for over 25 years. Agra Europe provides daily, week ly, monthly, and quarterly prices of key agricultural commodities dating back to 1997, and a platform where experts address specific questions raised by users of news and analysis produced by Agra Europe reporters and analysts.• Agra Europe provides information to ERS staff that helps in formulating models and analysis related to EU agricultural policy.• ERS staff have use Agra Europe articles for context and qualitative information on issues related geographical indicators, Agra Europe provided detailed information about the treatment of geographical indicators in the Canada-EU free trade agreement.• ERS staff use Agra Europe regularly to keep up to date on EU domestic and trade policy developments .• ERS reports on fruit and vegetable trade relied extensively on Agra Europe resources to document developments in the EU and other fruit and vegetable markets.• Agra Europe provides in-depth commodity and policy reports that are used extensively by ERS staff in the International Demand and Trade Branch, as well as staff who focus on specific commodities important to the U.S. trade.

Solicitation Title: Center for Nutrition Policy and Promotion (CNPP)
Description Text:
Center for Nutrition Policy and Promotion (CNPP)Nutrition Evidence Library (NEL) Operations, Maintenance (O&M) and Enhancement Support Services
This SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE is to determine the interest and technical capability of qualified businesses. This synopsis is for information and planning purposes and is not to be construed as a commitment by the U.S. Government, nor will the U.S. Government pay for information solicited. The information from this notice will help the United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA) Food and Nutrition Service (FNS) plan their acquisition strategy or plan.
CAPABILITY STATEMENT
FNS is seeking a contractor to perform both Operations & Maintenance (O&M) support, the development of new enhancement features and to provide technical consulting services for the Nutrition Evidence Library (NEL) systematic review portal and website system.
PLEASE NOTE THAT THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTE, NO SOLICITATION EXISTS AT THIS TIME. The Food and Nutrition Service, Center for Nutrition Policy and Promotion (CNPP) & Contracts Management Division (CMD), is conducting a market survey to determine the availability and technical capability of qualified businesses, including large and small business set-asides, small disadvantaged businesses (SDB), 8(a) small businesses, woman-owned small businesses (WOSB), veteran owned small businesses (VOSB), service-disabled veteran-owned small businesses (SDVOSB), Historically Under-utilized Business Zone (HUBZone) and Non-profit organization.
Offerors shall demonstrate their experience, skills, and qualifications with projects of similar scope and size completed within the last three (3) years, and explain in appropriate detail how they intend to demonstrate competency in each of the following points:
A. Demonstrate expertise in producing user friendly Web services to support systematic, transparent and replicable literature-based evidence synthesis products and services;B. Demonstrate expertise implementing systematic review Web services which track user progress and provide development transparency through a multi-step process;C. Demonstrate expertise in training for users on application changes, recommended user best-practices implementation and integrity risk, including NEL technical and application changes, statistical support for quantitative synthesis, copyediting and 508 compliance of web content, record-keeping and invoicing of abstractors;D. Demonstrate expertise in providing recommendations on the scientific synthesis methods and applied technology features for use in systematic review question development and refinement, literature scoping and screening, data extraction, risk of bias assessment, qualitative and quantitative evidence synthesis, and evidence grading;E. Experience in developing an integrated and secure web-based evidence analysis system as well as the ability to understand and troubleshoot a broad range of issues or problems related to NEL processes. Experience with implementing an electronic evidence-based synthesis system supporting a broad range of research designs, dedicated solely to systematically reviewing nutrition evidence in the United States. F. Manage nutrition systematic review projects of a similar size and scope, including meeting government objectives, ensuring the quality and timeliness of deliverables, and coordinating with the government funding agency.
The key objective of this project is:• Create and implement requested web development to improve the capabilities, functionalities, efficiency, and effectiveness of the NEL.• Provide system maintenance, hosting support, and updating the USDA's NEL portal and NEL public website, and ensure that they are fully functional, support current systematic review standards, and are user-friendly. These responsibilities include technical support with cloud hosting, project management for the application and database, operational guidance for scientific methodological integrity, development of enhancements and training support services, record-keeping and invoicing of abstractors copyediting and ensuring 508 compliance of materials. (www.NEL.gov or www.nutritionevidencelibrary.gov.
BACKGROUND ON THE NEL FUNCTIONS:The Nutrition Evidence Library (NEL) is a systematic review system within the USDA's Center for Nutrition Policy and Promotion that uses state-of-the-art methodology to search, evaluate, and synthesize food and nutrition-related research. CNPP's mission is to improve the health of Americans by developing and promoting dietary guidance that links scientific research to the nutrition needs of consumers. The NEL supports CNPP's mission by conducting systematic reviews to inform Federal nutrition policy and programs.
A NEL systematic review (SR) is used to evaluate scientific evidence on food and nutrition to answer a precise question or series of questions. In keeping with best SR practices, NEL systematic reviews are conducted by a multidisciplinary research team to: 1) develop systematic review questions2) search, screen, and select studies to review,3) extract data and assess the risk of bias of the research4) Describe and synthesize the evidence5) develop conclusion statements and grade the evidence,6) identify research recommendations. Each step of the process is documented in detail to ensure objectivity, transparency, and reproducibility of the process. CNPP has developed a web-based system called the NEL portal and website to provide the software and web support needed to implement this process to meet current systematic review standards.
This system has unique and secure features tailored to the requirements needed to support NEL systematic reviews such as:• Tailored web-based data extraction forms, data management with quality control of data, tailored data display capabilities, web-based forms which support dual completion and adjudication, with built in audit history of tracked changes, collaborative workspaces to upload and share documents, capabilities to support quantitative data synthesis, and public transparency to 508 compliant systematic review materials via a public website.• Project management tools for workflow support, task assignments and invoicing, scheduling of meetings, discussion boards, calendar and mail functions, metric dashboard, customizable reports, media library, and collaborative workspaces.• Uses ColdFusion version 10, along with IIS and SQL Server. The database size is under 5GB.SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS:Interested firms responding to this sources sought shall submit the following: Company name, address, point of contact, telephone number, email address, and Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) NAICS code. Provide business size and status such as Small Business, Disadvantaged, 8(a), Historically Underutilized Business Zones (HUB) Zone, Woman Owned, Minority Owned, Service Disabled Veteran Owned, or Other Than Small Business. Non-profit organization must indicate size (small or large business). Provide contract vehicles you participate in such as General Services Administration (GSA), National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Solutions for Enterprise Wide Procurement (SEWP), National Institutes of Health (NIH) Information Technology Acquisition and Assessment Center (NITAAC), or any other vehicle. Capability statement shall be 10 pages or less.
Please submit responses to this Sources Sought Notice electronically (via e-mail) to Keeyada Dickens, at keeyada.dickens@usda.gov and to Sean Merritt, at sean.merritt.usda.gov, no later than 4:00PM EST (Eastern Standard Time) on July 18, 2016. The e-mail subject line must specify Sources Sought for Nutrition Evidence Library (NEL). Facsimile responses will not be accepted.
NO TELEPHONE REQUESTS WILL BE ACCEPTED.

Description Text:
INTRODUCTION
This SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE is to determine the availability of potential small businesses (e.g., 8(a), service-disabled veteran owned small business, HUBZone small business, small disadvantaged business, veteran-owned small business, and women-owned small business) that can provide access to a database that can provide US business and household information that consist of current addresses, telephone numbers, firmographic, and demographic information. The database should provide information through a variety of media, including on-line reference and research tools and flat files for internal agency use.
The information from this market research will help USDA National Agricultural Statistic Service (NASS) plan their acquisition strategy. Please be sure to indicate if you have a GSA schedule contract, a contract on GSA 8(a) STARS, or a contract on GSA VETS GWAC.
THIS IS STRICTLY MARKET RESEARCH. NASS WILL NOT ENTERTAIN QUESTIONS REGARDING THIS MARKET RESEARCH.BACKGROUND
NASS uses statistical data to complete their agency's mission of providing timely, accurate, and useful statistics in service to U.S. agriculture. They require a database that they can access and collect the needed data and know it will be accurate.
RESPONSE INFORMATION
In order to respond to this notice, contractors must be able to clearly convey its experience and/or ability to perform the following tasks identified:
1. Access to a NAICS classification system
2. A map-based search option providing the user an ability to search by geo-codes.
3. Access to corporate linkage which allows for research up and down corporate ownership ladder
4. A very accurate database
5. A database that can be accessed electronically that includes US business and household information that consist of current addresses, telephone numbers, firmographic, and demographic information. As well as company employment size.
6. A file that can provide additional information on race, gender, and geocodes.7. Requires that each business contain a unique identifying number.Each response should include the following Business Information:
a. DUNS.b. Company Name.c. Company Address.d. Current GSA Schedules appropriate to this Sources Sought.f. Type of Company (i.e., small business, 8(a), woman owned, veteran owned, etc.) as validated via the System for Award Management (https://www.sam.gov/portal/public/SAM/) g. Company Point of Contact, Phone and Email address
Teaming Arrangements: All teaming arrangements should also include the above-cited information and certifications for each entity on the proposed team. Teaming arrangements are encouraged.
Responses must be submitted not later than 1/4/2016 , 12PM MST. Capability statements will not be returned and will not be accepted after the due date. The maximum number of pages for submission is 8 pages.
GENERAL INFORMATION
This Sources Sought Notice is for information and planning purposes only and should not be construed as a commitment by the Government. This is not a solicitation announcement for proposals and no contract will be awarded from this Notice. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this Notice. Respondents will not be notified of the results of this evaluation. Capability statements will not be returned and will not be accepted after the due date.
As previously stated, the Government will not entertain questions regarding this Market Research; however, general questions may be forwarded to the following:

Description Text:
The USDA Farm Service Agency, Aerial Photography Field Office and other USDA agencies require photogrammetric scanning services of primarily black and white 9x9 aerial film exposures from rolls. Scanning of color negative, positive, and infrared may be required as well. The date of film ranges from 1954 to 1992 with an average of 172 exposures per roll. Contract deliverables include the scanned images in TIF format, metadata files, film inspection reports, and progress reports.
1. This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items prepared in accordance with the format in Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; proposals are being requested and a written solicitation will not be issued.
2. Solicitation AG-8447-S-15-0005 is issued as a Request for Proposal (RFP)
3. The solicitation document and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through Federal Acquisition Circular 2005-83, dated August 3, 2015.
4. This solicitation is a Total Small Business Set Aside (FAR 52.219-6). The small business industry size standard for the type of services covered by this procurement, under NAICS code 518210, is the average annual receipts of the concern and its affiliates for the preceding three (3) years not in excess of $32.5 million.
5. Contract Items:
a. Scanned image TIFF files and metadata files. Quantities to be determined by task orders placed under IDIQ contract. The first task order is attached for between 240,000 and 325,000 black and white film exposures
b. Option for TIFF World File for orientation of image files
6. Photogrammetric scanning of large format aerial film, 9"x9" exposures; average of 175 exposures per roll; black and white, color, and infrared film types. Description Text:
THIS NOTICE OF INTENT IS NOT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS. This notice is for informational purposes only and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government. In accordance with FAR 6.302-1 and 41 USC 25(c)(1), the service needed by the USDA is available from only one responsible source and no other type of service will satisfy the needs of the USDA. This notice is not a request for competitive quotations. No solicitation document is available and telephone requests will not be honored. Potential sources who believe they can provide the services listed are invited to submit substantiating documentation in writing to the identified point of contact. Information received will be considered solely for the purposes of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. Oral communications are not acceptable in response to this notice. A determination by the Government to compete this proposed contract based on responses to this notice is solely at the discretion of the Government. The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), Economic Research Service (ERS), intends to award a sole source contract to Agra Europe for global coverage of food and agricultural policies, trade, WTO issues, data, and summaries of analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade. The Point of Contact for this action is Ms. Marian Dixon-Durham and any response must be emailed to Marian.Dixon-Durham@ars.usda.gov. Verbal responses are not acceptable and will not be considered. Interested parties may identify their interest and capability to respond to this requirement no later than July 4, 2015.
The NAICS Code for this requirement is 518210 Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
1.0 Introduction
ERS plays a strategic role in developing the USDA Baseline doing research and analysis of the implications to U.S. agriculture and food security of shocks both in the United States and around the world. In undertaking such a role, the analysts at ERS need to draw on a variety of data from outside of the USDA. One such tool required is a comprehensive system of economic and financial data, such as the Agra Europe system. Such a tool, which can be used on a desktop computer, facilitates doing a variety of analyses. ERS does not have the internal resources to develop and maintain such a data system. It is therefore necessary that we purchase such from an outside vendor.
2.0 Background
ERS publishes monthly and quarterly reports containing consistent U.S. and international nonagricultural data with a data history from the inception of the data source. The monthly forecast and reports about history require documentation to support ongoing reports and staff analysis done for the Secretary of Agriculture and other policymakers. The data needs to be developed on a basis consistent with the source entity to assure reliability in performing complex analyses of policy decisions, as well as reflect the interdependence of individual countries in the global market in Windows-based PC software such as Microsoft Office. The organization providing this has to have at least a 25 year track record
3.0 Scope
This Statement of Work covers the provision of data from July 24, 2015 - July 23, 2016.
The agency's main objectives for this service is to improve its function in providing the USDA with analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade from a European perspective. In depth information and analysis of European agriculture are available daily, covering commodities, policies, and topical issues such as the environment, food safety, ecology, biofuels, climate change, animal disease and welfare, water shortages, weather, and other topics of interest.
For these activities, we need:
1) A Windows-compatible global database system that is usable in generating tables, graphs, and spreadsheets. The database system needs to be installable on standard PCs, and the ERS LAN with the ability for users to look at data both graphically and in spreadsheet format to be manipulated by users. In addition, the data should be well documented.
2) The database needs to be available within an hour of release by the source entity to ERS and electronically via the internet including the updated historical data set used in outlook and research. This data set needs to be updated as new data become available.
3) Training, documentation, and technical support for these products and services. This includes up to 2 training sessions at ERS's discretion.
4.0 Required Data
Agra Europe provides comprehensive global coverage of food and agricultural policies, trade, WTO issues, data, and summaries of analyses and policies important to U.S. food and agricultural trade-from a European perspective. In depth information and analysis of European agriculture are available daily, covering commodities, policies, and topical issues such as the environment, food safety, ecology, biofuels, climate change, animal disease and welfare, water shortages, weather, and other topics of interest.
Examples of research where Agra Europe was used and was the only source of information available include the following:
• Agra Europe is an online intelligence service for the European Union's Common Agricultural Policy, as well as providing expert coverage of global agricultural policy, legislation and trade in farm commodities. Agra Europe is a well-established source of information, providing ERS staff with information to assist in informing agriculture professionals, officials, and policy makers for over 25 years. Agra Europe provides daily, weekly, monthly, and quarterly prices of key agricultural commodities dating back to 1997, and a platform where experts address specific questions raised by users of news and analysis produced by Agra Europe reporters and analysts. • Agra Europe provides information to ERS staff that helps in formulating models and analysis related to EU agricultural policy. • ERS staff have use Agra Europe articles for context and qualitative information on issues related geographical indicators, Agra Europe provided detailed information about the treatment of geographical indicators in the Canada-EU free trade agreement. • ERS staff use Agra Europe regularly to keep up to date on EU domestic and trade policy developments.• ERS reports on fruit and vegetable trade relied extensively on Agra Europe resources to document developments in the EU and other fruit and vegetable markets.• Agra Europe provides in-depth commodity and policy reports that are used extensively by ERS staff in the International Demand and Trade Branch, as well as staff who focus on specific commodities important to the U.S. trade.
